races.race_name: Celebrating 10 Years Of Tapeta At Newcastle Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Edwardtheninth
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's been turned over as favourite on four of his last six starts, two of them over C&D (behind Angelardo in March), and also behind Lady Phoebe at Pontefract; will be happier back on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Favourite when behind Lady Phoebe at Pontefract; will be happier back on Tapeta

runners.name: Star Of Jupiter
runners.spotlight: Inclined to pull hard in this headgear combination for the first time on his comeback and that was a solid effort all things considered (Nymphaea fourth); always the danger he'll do the same under Hope Regan (0-16) but claims if settling.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard on his comeback and that was a solid effort; claims if settling

runners.name: Nymphaea
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's found her level since cheekpieces went on, going close in similar races a couple of times earlier in the year; this the first time she's gone beyond 1m6f and she has claims if her stamina holds.
runners.comment: Maiden who's found her level since cheekpieces went on; claims if her stamina holds

runners.name: Lady Phoebe
runners.spotlight: Not achieved much in previous visits here but always over shorter and she's been found the right race having won comfortably over the extended 2m5f at Pontefract four weeks ago; holds obvious claims upped 5lb.
runners.comment: Won comfortably over the extended 2m5f at Pontefract four weeks ago; obvious claims up 5lb

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but likes it here (C&D winner in March off 4lb lower) and should fare better, the blinkers experiment having been binned following a below-par effort last time.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but likes it here (C&D winner in March off 4lb lower) and should fare better

runners.name: Ebendi
runners.spotlight: Useful in Ireland in his younger days but been poor under both codes for this yard for a good while; tailed off behind two of these at Pontefract last time.
runners.comment: Useful in his younger days but been poor under both codes for this yard for a good while

runners.name: Stitching Wheel
runners.spotlight: Trainer not saddled a Flat winner in around six years; this maiden has tumbled down the handicap since joining from Andre Fabre and needs to improve on this spring's turf efforts.
runners.comment: Tumbled down the handicap since joining from France; has to improve on recent turf efforts

races.race_name: Total Performance Data - 10 Years At Newcastle Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Littlecote
runners.spotlight: Well held in sole 2yo run but she's finished runner-up in three starts this spring and was only narrowly denied when odds-on at Wolverhampton (1m4f) three weeks ago; had steering issues (hanging right) in that race but she kept on well and pulled a long way clear of the third; sets the standard on that form and her RPRs have been progressive.
runners.comment: Runner-up in last three starts, including a close call at Wolverhampton latest; key player

runners.name: Ticker Tape
runners.spotlight: Time Test filly who has a smart pedigree; only third of four on Wolverhampton debut (8.6f) in March but she found plenty of progress when fourth of 12 at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack; 5-4 favourite) last month; that form puts her firmly in the picture and she's open to more progress at this new trip.
runners.comment: Promising fourth at Lingfield and she's open to more progress at this new trip; respected

runners.name: Maneater
runners.spotlight: Golden Horn half-sister to seven winners; promising second of four on Wolverhampton debut (9.4f) in October but she failed to build on that when 8l third here (1m2f) the following month; still early days but this 4yo needs to find more at this new trip on her return.
runners.comment: Placed twice in the autumn but it was a backward step latest; needs to find more on return

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: Well held in two small-field events on AW (1m4f) this spring and slow starts have been an issue; has plenty to find back on Tapeta and will be more interesting when sent down the handicap route.
runners.comment: Well held in two small-field events on AW this spring and handicaps will be more suitable
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Danseuse
runners.spotlight: By Teofilo; first foal; dam French 1m2f/1m3f winner, sister to winners Ispolini (1m6f Group 3) and Vivionn (useful 7f-1m2f), out of US 1m2f Grade 2 winner from family of King George winner Nathaniel; looks the part on paper and yard is 21% in novice events this year.
runners.comment: Looks the part on paper and yard is 21% in novice events this year

runners.name: Hello Friend
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; Frankel half-sister to winners Hello You (6f/7f 2yo including AW/Group 2; RPR 108), Ajyaall (7f turf/8.6f AW; 97) and Lufricia (5f AW; 93); dam French 7f/7.5f winner, half-sister to 6.5f Group 1 winner Signs Of Blessing; has quite a bit of speed in pedigree but she starts out over 1m4f and market should guide.
runners.comment: Has quite a bit of speed in pedigree but she starts out over 1m4f and market should guide

runners.name: The Lakes
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big prices in three bumpers and same story in two Flat runs at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) this spring; cheekpieces are now added.
runners.comment: Tailed off in three bumpers and same story in two Flat runs this spring

races.race_name: Sunday Lunch At The Border Minstrel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lehbab Prince
runners.spotlight: 170,000euros yearling who's the second foal of a Listed-race runner-up (1m6f) in Ireland (first offspring an ordinary middle-distance maiden); trainer boasts a good record with 2yos here (12-34).
runners.comment: 170,000euros yearling out of a Listed-race runner-up (1m6f); trainer 12-34 with 2yos here

runners.name: Dorigo
runners.spotlight: 37,000euros yearling who's the first foal of an unraced mare; trainer had a couple of 2yos win first time out this spring (both started favourite under Kevin Stott) and the market will be the best guide with him.
runners.comment: 37,000euros yearling who's the first foal of an unraced mare; market the best guide

runners.name: Big Cigar
runners.spotlight: Yard yet to get rolling for the year with juveniles; this early-foaled son of their Listed-placed 2yo (6f) Barbanera is an interesting newcomer, having been picked up for £140,000 at the breeze-up sales recently.
runners.comment: Out of the yard's Barbanera; picked up for £140,000 at the breeze-up sales recently

runners.name: Martial Order
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros foal, 75,000gns yearling who holds a few notable entries later on; half-sister Gold Digger won on her 2yo debut this time last year and he's an interesting contender for a yard that's 2-3 with 2yos on the AW this spring.
runners.comment: 32,000euros foal, 75,000gns yearling; holds a few notable entries; interesting contender

runners.name: Caeruleus
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns yearling who's only just turned two; brother and half-brother both won in Ireland for Jessica Harrington but his yard wouldn't be noted for 2yo newcomers (4-21 with juveniles here) and the market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: 80,000gns yearling who's only just turned two; the market will be the best guide

runners.name: Capilano
runners.spotlight: Already-gelded 72,000gns yearling whose dam went close on her 2yo debut for Godolphin before regressing; trainer had a juvenile newcomer win early in the month but boasts an ordinary record with them on the AW in recent times.
runners.comment: Gelded 72,000gns yearling; yard has an ordinary record with 2yos on the AW in recent times

runners.name: Real Trouble
runners.spotlight: Already-gelded 105,000gns early-foaled first offspring of Group 3-placed 2yo She's Trouble; holds a couple of notable entries for a yard that's enjoyed a good spring with juveniles; a likely type.
runners.comment: Couple of notable entries for a yard that's enjoyed a good spring with 2yos; a likely type

races.race_name: In Loving Memory Of Yvonne Rush Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gold Digger
runners.spotlight: Came away with the the useful Awaken on her debut last spring and started a single-figure price for the Group 3 Albany Stakes next time; missed the kick and pulled hard that day (Awaken second), so it wans't a bad effort; absent for 11 months since.
runners.comment: Single-figure price for the Group 3 Albany Stakes when last seen; sets the standard

runners.name: Zain Primus
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two sprint winners who looked to find 7f streching him on his debut here in February; built on that when winning tidily at Kempton (6f), an effort that's been very well advertised since; open to further improvement under the penalty.
runners.comment: Kempton win very well advertised since; open to further improvement under the penalty

runners.name: Project Wallace
runners.spotlight: Gelded 75,000euros yearling who's a half-brother to two winners, including Felix Natalis (2yo), who improved once handicapping; would need to be well above average to make a successful debut.
runners.comment: Gelded 75,000euros yearling; would need to be above average to make a successful debut

runners.name: Spaceman
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 7f-1m winner who made a solid debut when dead-heating for second at Redcar last November (6f); the winner (Listed) and joint-second have both won this spring and he has potential.
runners.comment: Solid debut last November (6f); winner (Listed) and joint-second have both won this spring

runners.name: April Diamond
runners.spotlight: Big prices for two runs over 6f to date and will likely find life easier once handicapping after today.
runners.comment: Big prices for two runs over 6f to date and will likely find life easier once handicapping

runners.name: Roy Lane
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when last of six on his debut last May and hung left when beaten a long way on his comeback, having been gelded; handicaps are probably next on the agenda.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on his comeback, having been gelded; handicaps are probably next up
runners.owner: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson

runners.name: The Cyprus Pearl
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in two starts to date; expect more of the same.
runners.comment: Huge prices and well beaten in two starts to date; expect more of the same

runners.name: Qila
runners.spotlight: Not a lot went right in two runs, including over C&D on debut, last September and he's one to keep an eye on; maybe in handicaps after this, though, with him returning from eight months off having undergone wind surgery.
runners.comment: One to keep an eye on; maybe in handicaps after this, though, back from wind surgery

races.race_name: Book Say When Ladies Day Tickets Handicap

runners.name: Mystical Land
runners.spotlight: Not seen it out a couple of times over 6f on turf latterly; run well a few times over C&D and the handicapper has given him a chance switched from blinkers to a visor; trainer also saddles Golden Havana.
runners.comment: Handicapper has given him a chance switched from blinkers to a visor; possibilities

runners.name: Gillali Lass
runners.spotlight: May have needed her comeback from nine months off at Musselburgh a few weeks ago but that was a poor effort; has to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: May have needed her comeback from nine months off but that was a poor effort

runners.name: Golden Havana
runners.spotlight: Course regular whose latest fourth, back from a short break, has been boosted since by the front three all going on to win; C&D victory late last year came in first-time blinkers and he holds decent claims switched to a visor; yard also run Mystical Land.
runners.comment: Course regular whose latest fourth here has been boosted since; decent claims

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Couple of costly defeats in the winter and has finished held in two runs back in handicaps; yard enjoying a decent spell and a 3lb drop in the weights will help but she's looking exposed now.
runners.comment: Held in two runs back in handicaps; 3lb lower but she's looking exposed now

runners.name: Houndhill
runners.spotlight: Held in a couple of small-field 6f nurseries here in the winter before hanging left on his turf comeback; never involved at a big price down to 5f last time and is given a try in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Never involved at a big price down to 5f last time; given a try in first-time headgear
runners.owner: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins

runners.name: With Glory
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Iain Jardine last autumn for 7,000gns; two poor efforts for her new yard this spring and isn't obviously fancied despite being dropped 5lb.
runners.comment: Two poor efforts for her new yard this spring and isn't obviously fancied

runners.name: Brave Traveller
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f nursery here off 2lb lower late last year; below that level in two runs back and has been gelded since only beating one home down to this trip at Catterick six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won a 6f nursery here off 2lb lower late last year; gelded since last seen six weeks ago

runners.name: Our Hero Matty
runners.spotlight: Maiden who regressed during a busy first season, latterly in headgear; made the early running before dropping away on his turf comeback and others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Made the early running before dropping away on comeback and others have stronger claims

runners.name: Oasis Cover
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in three Tapeta runs in the winter and came home nicely on her turf/handicap debut back from a break off this mark recently; entitled to have come on for that and this stiffer 5f should suit; one to consider.
runners.comment: Entitled to have come on for her turf comeback; stiffer 5f should suit; one to consider
runners.owner: All About York Partnership And Partner

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: Ex-George Scott maiden who's been running fairly in turf handicaps this spring; had plenty of chances now but won't mind the return to the AW and looks set to go well again.
runners.comment: Plenty of chances now but won't mind the return to the AW and looks set to go well again

runners.name: Everydaysasaturday
runners.spotlight: Still to get involved in a finish (5f-7f) and has missed the kick the last twice; will need to get away much better dropping from 7f in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Missed the kick the last twice; will need to get away much better dropping from 7f

runners.name: Ryefield
runners.spotlight: Well backed at big prices over 6f here when last seen six months ago; best effort last season came after a break and it will be interesting to see if the money comes again down 3lb.
runners.comment: Well backed at big prices over 6f here when last seen six months ago; watch the market

races.race_name: Book Northumberland Plate Day Tickets Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Hen Party
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's been turned over a few times in novice company, including on her turf comeback a month ago; consistent enough, though, and should be competitive off this sort of mark now handicapping.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden who should be competitive off this sort of mark now handicapping

runners.name: Annie Edson Taylor
runners.spotlight: Yard among the winners of late and this mare made a pleasing C&D comeback early in the month, especially given she hit traffic; entitled to have come on for that first run in six months and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Pleasing C&D comeback early in the month for her in-form yard; goes on the shortlist

runners.name: Fizzy Cristal
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning a C&D nursery off 3lb lower last autumn; no more than a fair turf comeback six weeks ago but that was her first run in six months and the return here looks a plus.
runners.comment: No more than a fair turf comeback six weeks ago but the return here looks a plus

runners.name: Blue Nguru
runners.spotlight: Reared as the stalls opened on her turf comeback and that can be ignored; easy to back that day but being dropped 2lb won't hurt and it will have blown away the cobwebs.
runners.comment: Reared as the stalls opened on her turf comeback and that can be ignored; possibilities

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who'll find this easier than the handicaps she's contested previously; had plenty of chances now but is fine on Tapeta and is one to consider for a yard also responsible for Perfect Price.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; had plenty of chances now but is fine on Tapeta and is one to consider

runners.name: Perfect Price
runners.spotlight: Two ordinary efforts in Tapeta handicaps this spring, each time at a big price; first-time headgear will need to make a difference dropped 4lb and Maldevious looks her trainer's best chance.
runners.comment: Two ordinary efforts in Tapeta handicaps this spring, each time at big prices; headgear on

runners.name: Concert
runners.spotlight: Ex-Haggas filly who was said to have been unsuited by rain-softened ground when well below market expectations last time (taken out at Doncaster over the weekend after the going eased); should do better.
runners.comment: Unsuited by rain-softened ground last time; should do better at this level

runners.name: Tashi Delek
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when second in a soft-ground Yarmouth maiden last backend (eyeshield) and again in a hood on her comeback over 8.4f; has the two forms of headgear combined as she runs in a sprint and a handicap for the first time on her AW debut.
runners.comment: Runs in a sprint and a handicap for the first time on her AW debut; market useful

runners.name: Too Much
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who can miss the break and/or pull hard; does most racing now over further and didn't offer much on her comeback here last month without the usual hood (back on today).
runners.comment: Dual course winner who can miss the break and/or pull hard; didn't offer much on comeback

races.race_name: Newcastle Tapeta Enabling Champions Since 2016 Handicap

runners.name: Criminal Shore
runners.spotlight: Rare run at shy of 1m when not far behind three of these over C&D last month; generally consistent but has only won once and didn't build on that back on turf last time.
runners.comment: Rare run at shy of 1m when not far behind three of these over C&D last month; consistent

runners.name: Yaajooz
runners.spotlight: Went close off this mark over C&D last backend and a good test over this trip is fine; quiet on his turf comeback and only has a selling handicap win to his name but he's quite capable.
runners.comment: Good test over this trip is fine; quiet on his turf comeback but he's quite capable

runners.name: Lord Capulet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who's won five already this year, the first four over C&D; a 5lb rise for his latest turf victory under 5lb claimer Jack Nicholls demands more but such has been his rate of progress he may well be up to it.
runners.comment: Front-runner who's won five already this year; 5lb for latest turf victory demands more

runners.name: King's School
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, latterly after a five-month break off 4lb lower last August; another solid effort fresh when not far behind Lord Capulet and The Green Man four weeks ago and is 7lb better off with the winner; can miss the break but is considered.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; can miss the break but is considered after a solid comeback here

runners.name: Atmosphere
runners.spotlight: String of consistent efforts round Kempton since the visor went on but has crept up the weights in defeat and will need to find a bit more on his Tapeta debut; will rightly be popular back from six weeks off though.
runners.comment: Needs to find a bit more on his Tapeta debut but will be popular back from six weeks off

runners.name: The Green Man
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner who's been in good form here this spring; no obvious reason he won't go well again, having split Lord Capulet and King's School last month, but there's not much room for manoeuvre off this mark.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner who's been in good form here this spring; should go well again
runners.owner: The Dalby Family And P Kirby

runners.name: Rich Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Always the danger he'll fluff the start, as he did on his C&D comeback in March; not beaten far that day and is on his last winning mark but comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Always the danger he'll fluff the start; on his last winning mark but comes with risks

runners.name: Cusack
runners.spotlight: Nine course wins to his name, mainly in the early months of the year; 50-1 for his C&D return a month ago, when well behind a few of these, and needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: 50-1 for his C&D return, when well behind a few of these, and needs a big step forward

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who hasn't been running badly this year without getting the breaks; on a handy mark again and holds each-way claims into a stronger race.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who hasn't been running badly this year without the breaks; on handy mark

runners.name: Red Mirage
runners.spotlight: Four-time Polytrack winner who ran well below market expectations in two previous starts on Tapeta; back on his last successful mark but didn't build on a decent comeback effort last time.
runners.comment: Back on his last successful mark but didn't build on a decent comeback effort last time

runners.name: Ticktyboo
runners.spotlight: Regressed for Nigel Tinkler last year; having a first run at beyond sprints trips for his new yard, back from seven months off; doesn't make obvious appeal.
runners.comment: First run at beyond sprints trips for his new yard back from seven months off

runners.name: Blazing Son
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner who's had his moments at the stalls; some way behind three of these over C&D four weeks ago and only holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner who's had his moments at the stalls; only holds each-way claims

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Ex-Gosden maiden who's shown enough this spring to suggest he's on a competitive mark now; still to finish placed after nine runs, often at short prices, but no surprise to see him pop up; look for some support this time.
runners.comment: Still to finish placed after nine runs but no surprise to see him pop up; look for support
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Adamlyi
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt who seemed to need the outing/experience in sole 2yo appearance; went very close at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) on reappearance, despite still looking green, and the winner gave the form a boost by following up in Newbury handicap on Saturday; leading player at the weights.
runners.comment: Went close at Leicester on reappearance and the winner has franked the form since

runners.name: Runman
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a Group 2 scorer for his connections; two from two, both races at about 1m on good ground, latest at Epsom where he was a 1-12 shot and won going away; has potential and warrants respect, even with a double penalty, in his bid for a novice treble.
runners.comment: Two from two; respected, even with a double penalty, in his bid for a novice treble

runners.name: Lucky Camino
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal, 42,000gns yearling; Lucky Vega half-brother to four winners, notably Fanaar (6f-1m including UAE Group 3; RPR 106); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Indian Choice; market informative.
runners.comment: 42,000gns yearling; Lucky Vega half-brother to four winners; market informative

runners.name: Atlas Mountain
runners.spotlight: Similar level of form in maidens at Glorious Goodwood and Newbury, gelded prior to keeping-on tenth in big field over 1m latest; still open to improvement, albeit his ideal scene may be handicaps.
runners.comment: Still open to improvement, albeit his ideal scene may be handicaps

runners.name: Moreedd
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt whose siblings include his connections' 1,000 Guineas winner Elmalka; ran below expectations in 7f contest at Doncaster, finishing only fourth of five, but seems very likely to improve on that effort; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Well bred; ran below market expectations in Doncaster contest but should improve

runners.name: Lean d'Aislingean
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros foal, 85,000euros yearling; by Ghaiyyath; sibling to two winners, notably Swatch (1m-1m2f; RPR 78); dam unraced daughter of 5f Group 1 scorer; one of a few newcomers in the field; check the betting.
runners.comment: 85,000euros yearling; by Ghaiyyath; one of a few newcomers in the field

runners.name: Mr Bollinger
runners.spotlight: Something to find on his 1m2f efforts at Nottingham and Lingfield (AW) but gives the impression this drop in distance may prompt better form; gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Gelded since latest 1m2f effort; drop in trip looks worth exploring

runners.name: Peace And Quiet
runners.spotlight: Makes belated debut aged four; by New Approach and first foal of twice-raced half-sister to 1m4f/12.5f Group 2 scorer Loxley and daughter of 1m2f Group 1 winner; check the market signals.
runners.comment: Makes belated debut aged four; by New Approach; check the market signals

runners.name: Revelio
runners.spotlight: Ordinary RPRs in a couple of maidens 12 months apart, last of nine at Yarmouth (1m) for new yard latest; this 4yo seems likely to be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Ordinary RPRs in a couple of maidens 12 months apart; handicaps more suitable soon

runners.name: Florizelle
runners.spotlight: Ran in accordance with market expectations (rank outsider of five) in 7f AW event at Wolverhampton, achieving little on the figures.
runners.comment: Rank outsider and achieved little in 7f AW event

runners.name: Carly Minogue
runners.spotlight: 5yo mare who has recorded an RPR of just 53 in both starts, which where last June; best watched.
runners.comment: Recorded an RPR of just 53 in both starts, which were last June

races.race_name: Family Fun Raceday Sunday 31st May Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Outflank
runners.spotlight: Represents notable connections and has shown ability with a couple of third-place finishes, latest at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) where he was beaten only 2l and pulled clear of the remainder; major contender at the weights.
runners.comment: Clear third at Salisbury last time; major contender at the weights

runners.name: Knights Charge
runners.spotlight: Both outings on AW, finishing third to useful rival at Kempton (1m) then drawing 5l clear of closest pursuer when odds-on at Lingfield (1m2f); similar type to Bay Royale, being a last-time winner who may well progress further.
runners.comment: Scored by 5l at Lingfield in latest AW start; may well progress further; major player

runners.name: Bay Royale
runners.spotlight: Cost 220,000gns as a yearling; both starts at Nottingham, latest six weeks ago (1m2f, good) when building on his 2yo effort (1m) and scoring comfortably; this Irish Derby entry looks likely to improve further; respected under 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Both starts at Nottingham, scoring comfortably latest; Irish Derby entry; respected

runners.name: Cloud Forest
runners.spotlight: Blue Point half-brother to French 6f winner Dream Whisper (RPR 92); dam 5f 2yo scorer (99), closely related to 1m4f Group 1 winner Old Persian (1m4f Group 1); Godolphin runner who is making belated debut aged four; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Godolphin runner who is making belated debut; heed the market signals

runners.name: Zurna
runners.spotlight: 23,000gns 3yo; Sea The Moon half-brother to three winners, notably Siskany (1m-2m including US Grade 2; RPR 119); stable not associated with successful newcomers; best watched.
runners.comment: 23,000gns 3yo; stable not associated with successful newcomers

runners.name: Semele
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in bumpers and middle-distance AW maidens but the balance of form suggests she has something to find in this field; only a modest third at Wolverhampton most recently.
runners.comment: Balance of bumper/hurdles form suggests she has something to find
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs A E Pakenham

runners.name: Liam Mera Kai
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground bumper on debut then posted consistent efforts over hurdles until form dipped last time (handicap debut); nothing out of the ordinary and looks below the likely required standard on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Bumper/hurdles form suggests he's below the required standard on Flat debut

runners.name: Shopaholic
runners.spotlight: Finished about 8l fifth of eight in Lingfield maiden (1m2f, AW); may do better with that belated debut under her belt but looks unlikely to reverse placings with the winner, Knights Charge.
runners.comment: May do better but is unlikely to reverse Lingfield placings with Knights Charge

runners.name: Cliff Danger
runners.spotlight: From a family that has served his yard well but is probably one for the longer term; gelded since 40-1 sixth of eight in 1m race at Wetherby.
runners.comment: Gelded since 40-1 sixth of eight at Wetherby; longer-term prospect

runners.name: Barak Warrior
runners.spotlight: Only seventh in both runs (1m2f) and looks likely to find better opportunities in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Only seventh in both runs; handicaps more suitable shortly
runners.owner: W Sweatman, J Summers, R Foden

races.race_name: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap

runners.name: Stepanov
runners.spotlight: Best AW effort when second over 9.5f at Wolverhampton; gelded since last run (1m3f); bred to be suited by the switch to turf on handicap debut; may show progress.
runners.comment: Bred to be suited by the switch to turf on handicap debut; may show progress

runners.name: Sports Day
runners.spotlight: Placed in two races at Yarmouth (1m/1m2f) since handicapping, showing improved form, last time beating all bar a rival who was ahead of the assessor; solid chance.
runners.comment: Placed in two races at Yarmouth since handicapping, latest behind a well-treated rival

runners.name: Grindleton
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who is showing some consistency and ties in with Sports Day on Yarmouth (1m2f) reappearance form; may improve with that outing under his belt; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Showing some consistency; ties in with Sports Day on Yarmouth reappearance form

runners.name: Lady Lauren
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has been campaigned only at up to 1m and is one of the most exposed runners in this field; similar type to Mohaab.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden and similar type to Mohaab

runners.name: Veld
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive figures in AW events at about 1m; bred to be suited by this new trip on turf/handicap debut; another step forward looks plausible; one to consider.
runners.comment: Bred to be suited by this new trip on turf/handicap debut; another step forward is likely
runners.owner: Sir Richard Pilkington & Mrs S Rogers

runners.name: Aurelune
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who was only a modest sixth at Beverley (8.5f) switched to turf most recently; however, one of several runners who may do better now handicapping.
runners.comment: One of several runners who may do better now handicapping

runners.name: Labiche
runners.spotlight: Modest third Lingfield (1m, AW) on handicap/seasonal debut; may fare better with the outing under his belt but needs such progress in this deeper-looking affair.
runners.comment: Needs to build on reappearance effort; this looks a deeper race
runners.owner: T A Frost & Simon Broke & Partners

runners.name: Premier Cru
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has regressive results but may stage a turnaround now handicapping; debut effort, namely fifth on Kempton AW, was reasonably promising.
runners.comment: Has regressive results but may stage a turnaround now handicapping

runners.name: Houndswood Willow
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 12l in each of his 1m runs but this combination of new trip, first-time headgear and switch to handicap level may prompt a bigger effort.
runners.comment: May do better now handicapping, upped in trip and wearing headgear

runners.name: Thimble
runners.spotlight: From a family that has served her owner well; not the form pick on her 7f AW efforts but pedigree suggests she'll improve for this new trip on turf/handicap debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests she'll improve for this new trip on turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Mohaab
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of her form at 7f-1m; one of the most exposed contenders, being 0-9, and has something to prove back up in distance to boot.
runners.comment: One of the most exposed contenders, being 0-9

runners.name: Tenison
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who is far from solid on form and has something to prove off her opening mark; on the other hand, the effective drop in class may help her.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form and has something to prove off her opening mark

races.race_name: Events And Hospitality At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: North Force
runners.spotlight: Four placed efforts in the north reflect a consistent start to his career, good second in Beverley handicap (7.5f) most recently; related to a couple of winners for his owner and has shown enough to suggest he can emulate them; solid.
runners.comment: Has made a consistent start to his career and holds solid claims

runners.name: Capichera
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly whose form stacks up well; creditable third to useful sort (Listed placed since) in 1m maiden at the Newmarket Craven meeting most recently; open to progress in handicaps and appears to have a workable opening mark; respected.
runners.comment: Form stacks up well; appears to have a workable opening mark; respected

runners.name: Domination
runners.spotlight: Opened his account with neck success, first two clear, in Class 6 handicap at Ripon (1m) on stable/seasonal debut; upped two grades but may have more to offer and, getting weight all round, looks one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won at Ripon on stable/seasonal debut and may have more to offer

runners.name: Rosa Inglesa
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive RPRs over 7f; has performed well in Newmarket sales race (sixth of 26) and Wetherby handicap (close third) since recording an AW success; likely player, provided she stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive RPRs over 7f; likely player, provided she stays 1m

runners.name: Beyond The Bar
runners.spotlight: Showed steadily progressive maiden/novice form that culminated in Redcar success (1m); has failed to transfer it to two handicaps but possibly needed his reappearance run last time.
runners.comment: Has failed to transfer maiden/novice progress to two handicaps

runners.name: Island Bear
runners.spotlight: Gelded since finishing last of seven at Southwell (1m, AW) on handicap/seasonal debut; may do better returned to turf with the outing under his belt, having shown some promise in this sphere as a 2yo.
runners.comment: May do better returned to turf in this second handicap attempt

runners.name: I'm Just Ken
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden; always behind in Class 2 handicap at Chester (7.5f) most recently, having been one of the outsiders; has otherwise shown some ability and this grade should be more like his level.
runners.comment: Always behind in Class 2 last time; this should be more like his level

runners.name: Chapter
runners.spotlight: Still quite unexposed but may be ground-dependent; has shown his handicap form (win/third, both over 7f last autumn) on heavy and was withdrawn due to good going on intended reappearance; gelded since he last raced.
runners.comment: Still unexposed but has shown his handicap form on heavy ground

runners.name: Runswick
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo form when scoring at Epsom (7f) on penultimate start; not disgraced when facing stiffer task in Newmarket sales race next time; could go well, returned to calmer waters on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Could go well, returned to calmer waters on handicap/seasonal debut

races.race_name: Conferences And Events At Nottingham Racecourse Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Wondrous Light
runners.spotlight: In good form dropped back to about 1m this term, winning at Beverley then creditable fourth at Southwell (AW); still unexposed over this sort of trip; shortlisted.
runners.comment: In good form dropped to about 1m this term; still unexposed over this sort of trip

runners.name: Pretty Spirited
runners.spotlight: Maiden prior to posting form figures of 1133 in last four appearances, close third at Doncaster (1m) on seasonal debut latest; upped in grade but may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Close third at Doncaster on reappearance; upped in grade but may remain competitive
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Saliko
runners.spotlight: Bit to prove back at 1m but is otherwise interesting returned to Nottingham, being 2-2 here, namely a 1m2f double last July; 0-10 elsewhere.
runners.comment: 2-2 at Nottingham, namely a 1m2f double last July; interesting back here

runners.name: Stella Lucente
runners.spotlight: Consistent efforts over 7f, most recently third at Southwell (AW; handicap debut) despite a stumble; likely player in this second turf attempt, provided she stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Consistent efforts over 7f; likely player, provided she stays new trip

runners.name: Volendam
runners.spotlight: Heavy defeat last time took her AW record to 0-15; opened her account with comfortable win at Bath (1m) earlier in April and remains unexposed on turf; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Opened her account with comfortable win at Bath in most recent turf attempt

runners.name: Porth Eilian
runners.spotlight: Siblings include a useful performer for her connections; gradually getting the hang of things; looks interesting on handicap debut, with another step forward plausible.
runners.comment: Siblings include a useful performer for her connections; interesting on handicap debut
runners.owner: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner

runners.name: Espanita
runners.spotlight: Raced four times, all over 7f on AW; disappointing favourite in two handicaps since winning a maiden; new scenario needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Disappointing in last two 7f AW attempts; new scenario needs to make a difference

runners.name: Ziata
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in all three starts, most recently runner-up at Newcastle (1m, AW) on seasonal debut; open to progress in handicaps and looks reasonably solid.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in all three starts; open to progress in handicaps; reasonably solid

runners.name: Pretty Ambitious
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice for previous yard; down the field at Lingfield (AW) on stable/handicap/seasonal debut but may do better with the outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Down the field in AW event on stable/handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Galileo Charm
runners.spotlight: Has broadly consistent 7f AW form that features a success in March; failed to transfer it to turf in C&D event last time; something to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer her 7f AW form to turf in C&D event last time

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-5 since handicapping but she's in a consistent vein of form that includes a good third over C&D on penultimate start; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: In a consistent vein of form; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear

runners.name: Katalyst
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who still has no placed form; chance largely depends on the effects of new headgear, returned to turf for first time since debut.
runners.comment: Chance largely depends on the effects of new headgear

runners.name: Huggable
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who showed her best form (one win, two seconds) last year over 1m2f; bit to prove back at 1m but otherwise may do better still.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly; showed best form (one win, two seconds) over 1m2f

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Sonic Si
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden but he's reached the frame in sprint handicaps on AW in his last four starts; remains unexposed on turf and he could go well again off a sliding mark back in this sphere.
runners.comment: 0-13 but he's been running well on AW and could be in the mix again back on turf

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice on Tapeta (5f/6f) this year but he's been well below form on AW in his last two runs; overall record is now 0-12 and he has something to prove back on turf; new headgear.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who has been well below form on AW in last two runs; new headgear

runners.name: Mister McGregor
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 23-race maiden who has finished out the back at Wolverhampton and Thirsk in his last two runs; visor is now tried and he needs a major revival on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: 23-race maiden who has finished out the back in his last two runs; visor now tried

runners.name: Timebar
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but he was runner-up in a classified event here (6f, good) in his final run last season; returns after 341 days off but he should be fine back at this trip and he needs watching in market.
runners.comment: 0-15 but was runner-up here in final run last season; needs watching in market on comeback

runners.name: Poetic Jack
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner (6f/7f) but his last success was 17 months ago and he's been up and down in five runs this year; ran too freely at Lingfield last time but that was a disappointing run as favourite and he needs to bounce back again after that.
runners.comment: His last win was 17 months ago and he's been up and down this year; others preferred

runners.name: Masterclass
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run in this race last year but he's 0-15 since and has been hard to predict; now 3lb lower than for that last success but he faded at Doncaster last time and he comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last May but he's 0-15 since and has been hard to predict; down the list

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Snow
runners.spotlight: Has reached the frame on several occasions for current yard but she was beaten 5l when third at Southwell (5f, AW) last month and that took her overall record to 0-18; others look stronger.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden and she was well held when third on AW latest; others look stronger

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Recorded his fifth win when justifying favouritism under a prominent ride at Catterick (5f, good) last Monday; a switch to a 7lb claimer helps to offset most of the penalty for that success and he won twice under Harry Smith last summer; big player.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Catterick last week and he's a big player again under a penalty

runners.name: Chasing Gold
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 13 starts but he was generally consistent in sprint handicaps last year; returns after five months off but he's well handicapped on his best form and needs a close look back on turf.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden but he was consistent in handicaps last year; needs a close look on return

runners.name: Bang On The Bell
runners.spotlight: His last win was at Windsor 13 months ago but that was off 13lb higher and he finished a close third over C&D (good to firm) on his penultimate run; has possibilities if he can recapture that form and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and has claims on his close third over C&D on penultimate run

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden and his first four runs were for Andrew Balding; perked up with a second in new equipment at Wolverhampton (5f) last month but he failed to back that up when last of eight at Lingfield; hood now returns and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Inconsistent eight-race maiden and he was last of eight at Lingfield last time

runners.name: Diamondsinthesand
runners.spotlight: All of his four turf wins have been on good or slower including heavy; his last win was at Bath (5.6f) last September but he's finished down the field there in his last three runs; now drops back in grade but he needs to rediscover his spark; regular hood is removed.
runners.comment: His last win was at Bath (5.6f) but he's finished down the field there in last three runs

runners.name: Dunnington Lad
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 5f at Ripon in 2024 but last year was a struggle and he was beaten 11l on his Redcar reappearance (6f, good) 12 days ago; now drops back in trip but he has lots to prove and needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Last year was a struggle and he reappeared with an 11l defeat at Redcar; opposable

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: All of his 18 runs have been on turf and he won three handicaps (5f/6f) on good ground last spring; his last success was off 6lb higher but he's been out of sorts in his last six runs (can be excused latest) and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: 4yo who has been out of sorts in last six runs and he needs to get back on track

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Further Measure
runners.spotlight: Held on to win at Kempton (2m, AW) in February and posted a solid effort when fourth at Southwell (2m, AW) 84 days ago; off since and this drop back in trip probably isn't ideal on his first outing on turf since September 2023.
runners.comment: Won over 2m at Kempton in February; returns from 84 days off and now drops back in trip

runners.name: King's Castle
runners.spotlight: Won at Ffos Las (1m2f, good to soft) and Sandown (1m2f, good) last summer, but he wasn't able to build on those victories in three subsequent starts; absent since October but has place claims on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Won twice over 1m2f last summer; below his best in three subsequent outings; place claims

runners.name: Hengest
runners.spotlight: Built on the promise of last month's Epsom (8.5f, good) fourth when making a winning handicap debut at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) eight days ago; this longer trip should be fine (dam won over 1m7f) and he's the one to beat under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Made a winning handicap debut at Windsor (1m2f) last week; one to beat under a penalty
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing, P Gleeson & N Barnes

runners.name: Upepo
runners.spotlight: Well backed when winning here on the AW over 1m4f in December but never featured when beating only one rival home 19 days ago; 0-9 on turf and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Well backed when winning here on the AW in December; 0-9 on turf and others are preferred

runners.name: Atlantic Sunset
runners.spotlight: Visored for the first time when scoring at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) last June for his previous yard, but he was well beaten on his yard debut at Yarmouth three weeks ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won in a first-time visor at Newmarket last year; well beaten at Yarmouth on stable debut

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Five wins from 43 starts, the most recent at Southwell (1m6f, AW) last January off a mark 10lb higher than today's; bounced back from a disappointing run when fourth of six at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 17 days ago and has place claims.
runners.comment: 10lb below his last winning mark and ran respectably at Doncaster last time; place claims

runners.name: Big Bear Hug
runners.spotlight: Made a winning stable debut at Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) last June but she's now 12lb lower following some lesser efforts; better is needed but any rain will suit.
runners.comment: Below her best recently but now 12lb lower than when winning on last year's stable debut

runners.name: Bridge
runners.spotlight: Both of his wins have been over hurdles, the most recent when rewarding market support at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) 53 days ago; returns to the Flat for the first time since July 2023 with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Dual winner over hurdles, including when well supported last time; back on the Flat

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Vidmiyr
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in three starts in Ireland and was beaten only a neck at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on his stable debut in April; had to wait for a run and would have gone a bit closer had he not been short of room at the finish when third in a handicap at Doncaster (1m, good to firm), and has good claims back in maiden, though ground slower than good would be an unknown.
runners.comment: Went close on his yard debut and unlucky not to go a bit closer last time; good claims
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Spirit Of Albion
runners.spotlight: 0-8 for Amanda Perrett but he has been placed on three occasions, including when going close at Kempton (7f, AW) in November 2024 on his third start; below his best when last seen 293 days ago and now makes his stable debut having been purchased for 9,000gns; watch the betting.
runners.comment: 0-8 for Amanda Perrett but has placed on three occasions; yard debut (bought for 9,000gns)

runners.name: Ice To Meet You
runners.spotlight: 4yo by Postponed; second foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; RPR 91), half-sister to winners Delicate Kiss (1m) and Kiss And Run (5f including 2yo), out of useful 1m-1m2f winner; hooded for his belated debut.
runners.comment: 4yo by Postponed; dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; RPR 91); hooded for his belated debut

runners.name: Fiefdom
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros foal, £25,000 yearling; gelded; sixth foal; closely related to Italian 7f/1m winner Paparemobeach, half-brother to winners Thunderey (Scandinavian 6f-1m including 2yo) and Donn Clifs (French/German/Czech 7.5f/1m); dam unraced sister to 1m2f winner Tenure; watch the market on debut.
runners.comment: 5,000euros foal, £25,000 yearling; closely related to Italian 7f/1m winner Paparemobeach

runners.name: Squashy Berry
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in two starts over 6f, most recently at Kempton (AW) 15 days ago; steps up in trip and he'll likely be of more interest when entering handicaps.
runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten in two starts over 6f; likely one for handicaps

runners.name: Malakai Kite
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish where he did after blowing the start on his debut at Bath (1m, good to soft; 28-1) 32 days ago; drop in trip may not be ideal on that evidence but he's open to improvement, particularly if exiting the stalls better.
runners.comment: Did well to finish where he did after missing the break on his debut at Bath (1m)

runners.name: Ocean Force
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has been well held in 1m maidens at Kempton (AW) and Bath (good to soft) last month; drops back in trip and is best watched.
runners.comment: Well held in 1m maidens at Kempton and Bath last month; drops in trip and is best watched

runners.name: Sayidah Aleen
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners inc Gifts Of Gold (1m1f-1m7f including AW; RPR 114), Dusky Queen (7f including 2yo/Listed; 110) and Achnaha (8.6f/1m3f including US; 98); last of four on her debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in March for Jack Channon and has since moved yards having been bought for 15,500gns; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Bought for 15,500gns since finishing last of four on debut; cheekpieces on for yard debut

runners.name: Ernie McCrew
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 6f winner Babe Alicious (including AW; RPR 61); hinted at ability when behind Vidmiyr on his debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) 22 days ago but may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability when behind Vidmiyr on his debut at Wolverhampon; may be one for later

races.race_name: Simon Duggan The Bad Ama Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Whiteley Way
runners.spotlight: 0-21 (Flat/hurdles) but went close over 1m after missing the break at Kempton in March; didn't get home having failed to settle over 1m2f at Bath (firm) 20 days ago but should be happier over this shorter trip; tongue-tie returns and she won't mind any rain (runner-up in both starts on ground slower than good to soft)
runners.comment: 0-21 but went close over 1m at Kempton in March; failed to settle over 1m2f last time

runners.name: Banksman
runners.spotlight: Both of his wins were in classified events when trained by Jack Channon; well beaten in two starts on the flat since joining this stable and he was pulled up over hurdles 30 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Dual winner for Jack Channon; struggled for this yard and pulled up over hurdles latest

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Gained his first win on turf when landing a handicap at Bath (1m, good) this month and followed up there over 1m2f (firm; made all) six days ago; holds good claims dropping back in trip under a 4lb penalty but any rain probably won't be ideal.
runners.comment: Won his last two starts at Bath (1m/1m2f); drops back in trip under a penalty; good claims

runners.name: Mr Fustic
runners.spotlight: Six wins from 37 starts, the most recent success coming at Southwell (1m, AW) in March; ran respectably here on the AW 19 days ago, taking his form figures since the visor was applied to 314, but he's 0-7 on turf.
runners.comment: 6-37; won at Southwell in March and ran respectably here on the AW latest; 0-7 on turf

runners.name: Maury
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who finished unplaced in his first 11 starts, but made a winning reappearance here on the AW in March; posted another solid effort at Wolverhampton (7f, AW; beaten 1l) 11 days ago and has claims if handling this switch back to turf.
runners.comment: Won here on the AW in March and posted a solid effort last time; claims if handling turf

runners.name: Huntly Lodge
runners.spotlight: 0-12; beaten only 2l when fifth of 11 here over 7f in a classified event in February but he's not backed that up in two starts since; well held in five starts on turf and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Disappointing on his last two starts and he's yet to show much on turf; others preferred

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts came at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last May for Pam Sly; faded late having set a strong pace over 1m a Yarmouth last month and ran a solid race when third there over 6f three weeks ago; contender back up in trip.
runners.comment: Sole win came over 7f; posted some solid efforts recently (6f/1m) and she's a contender

runners.name: Weston Court
runners.spotlight: Both of his wins came in the summer of 2024 (good to firm/firm) but he's mostly struggled since; failed to beat a rival home in two last last month and needs the switch of headgear to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Both wins in the summer of 2024 but has mostly struggled since; blinkers need to help

runners.name: Letsbeatsepsis
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground slower than good to soft; 0-12 but he's been runner-up on two occasions, including here over 1m2f last August (good to firm); too free here over 1m4f on the AW when last seen 258 days ago and drops back in trip on his reappearance.
runners.comment: 0-12 but has been runner-up on two occasions; too free on the AW over 1m4f when last seen

runners.name: Senseofentitlement
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 23 starts came at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in July 2023 for his previous yard; mostly struggled since joining this stable in 2024 but did shaped more encouragingly when fifth of eight at Kempton (1m, AW) 55 days ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Shaped more encouragingly at Kempton in March and now needs to build on that; back on turf
runners.owner: Miss M P Bryant, David & Eileen Bryant

runners.name: Mighty Ruler
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 19 starts came here on the AW over 7f in January and she ran well here over 6f 19 days ago, keeping on into third to do best of those from off the pace; step back up in trip will suit but he's yet to be placed in four starts on turf.
runners.comment: 1-19; ran well over 6f last time and this step back up in trip will suit; 0-4 on turf

runners.name: Up The Anti
runners.spotlight: Won at Bath (5.5f, heavy) and Kempton (6f, AW) last year, but she beat only one rival home at Kempton last month following a 63-day break; has won over 1m so this step back up in trip will be no issue, and she won't be inconvenienced by any rain.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival home following a short break last time; won't mind the forecast rain

runners.name: Oviedo
runners.spotlight: Zetland Gold Cup winner at Redcar off a mark of 96 in 2023 when trained by Ed Bethell, but he struggled in 2024 for that stable and its been the same story since joining Alexandra Dunn; mark continues to tumble and cheekpieces are now tried on his first start for 116 days.
runners.comment: Continues to tumble in the weights but has struggled for this yard; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Heer's Sadie
runners.spotlight: All six of her wins have been at either Brighton or Yarmouth, and she's been off since finishing fifth of 12 at the former track in October; handles most underfoot conditions but may need this return.
runners.comment: All six wins at either Brighton or Yarmouth; handles most conditions but may need this

races.race_name: Simon Duggan The Bad Ama Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came in Jersey (1m, firm) last summer; four races for this yard, back to form when only just denied in 16-runner race at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) three days ago; hung left on that occasion and rider briefly stopped riding, but this 4yo has solid claims today.
runners.comment: Sole win came in Jersey last year; hung left and just denied at Doncaster three days ago

runners.name: The Spotlight Kid
runners.spotlight: Both wins on turf have been at Yarmouth over 7f and he's also won twice on the AW; went close at Southwell (1m, AW) in January but he did too much too soon there 20 days ago; claims if settling better.
runners.comment: Both wins on turf have been over 7f at Yarmouth; did too much too soon at Southwell latest

runners.name: Joycean Way
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; runner-up on five occasions, most recently on soft ground at Brighton (1m) in September, but below his best on his last two starts and needs to bounce back; headgear removed.
runners.comment: 0-16 but runner-up five times; below his best on last two outings; headgear removed
runners.owner: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

runners.name: Dubai Harbour
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been here on the AW over 1m2f; ran well when runner-up at Kempton (1m, AW) in March but well beaten there 55 days ago and he's struggled in each of his three starts on turf.
runners.comment: Both wins have been here on the AW over 1m2f; well beaten last time and turf is a concern

runners.name: Charlatan
runners.spotlight: Gained his fifth AW success when winning at Wolverhampton in December but in and out since; rallied for fourth after losing his position at Southwell (7f, AW) 69 days ago; lightly raced on turf and unproven on softer than good.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner; solid fourth at Southwell last time; unproven on softer than good

runners.name: Drafted
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner, most recently at Dundalk (7f, AW) in October when trained by Charles Byrnes; third on his stable debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in February but he's not built on that in three starts since; unraced on ground slower than good to soft.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner; third at Chelmsford on stable debut but has not built on that

runners.name: Resilient Lady
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden (Flat/hurdles) who has yet to be placed, but it was a respectable run when fourth of nine at Kempton (1m, AW) 55 days ago; struggled in her two starts on Flat turf.
runners.comment: 0-13; respectable effort at Kempton 55 days ago but struggled in both starts on Flat turf

runners.name: Celtic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Well held in three novice events last year (6f/7f) and he failed to beat a rival on handicap debut at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) 24 days ago; drops back in trip; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well held in three novice events and didn't beat a rival over 1m2f on his handicap debut

runners.name: Snow Boots
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who posted one of his better efforts when midfield at Kempton (6f, AW) 76 days ago; steps up in trip on his first start since.
runners.comment: 0-16 but posted one of his better efforts at Kempton over 6f in March; steps up in trip

runners.name: Bear To Dream
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, with five of those wins coming on turf; ran well when second of 11 here on the AW in December and switches to turf on her first start since; seems unsuited by ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Six-time winner; off since 2nd here on AW in January; won't want ground softer than good

runners.name: Raqraaq
runners.spotlight: Dual winner here on the AW over 1m; bounced back from a couple of lesser efforts when third of nine here on the AW over 7f last time but he could do without the rain on this switch back to turf (well beaten in three starts on soft).
runners.comment: Dual winner here on the AW over 1m; bounced back last time from a couple of lesser efforts

runners.name: Turpin
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who posted his best effort in handicaps when fifth of 12 here (1m, AW) 19 days ago on his first start since being gelded; needs to build on that returned to turf.
runners.comment: 0-7; fifth of 12 here on the AW last time and needs to build on that returned to turf

runners.name: Kev
runners.spotlight: Went close in a maiden at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in December 2024 but he's struggled in handicap company since; visor (worn last time) is replaced by first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Went close in a maiden in 2024 but disappointing in four handicaps since; blinkers on

runners.name: Reel Power
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a classified event at Brighton (1m, good to firm) in July 2023; fair fourth at Kempton in September but down the field in three starts since; back on turf.
runners.comment: Sole win came at Brighton in July 2023 and down the field on his last three starts

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Thanos
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in six starts but runner-up in each of his three starts in handicap company, most recently at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; gelded) on his return 24 days ago; contender with cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Runner-up in each of his three starts in handicap company; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Based
runners.spotlight: Down the field in all three starts as a 2yo but shaped well on his handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW; gelded) 22 days ago, coming home nicely for second despite going wide on the turn for home; makes his turf debut and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Shaped well when second on his handicap debut at Wolverhampton; on the shortlist

runners.name: Personal Pride
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly when a close third of eight at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in December on final qualifying run but failed to beat a rival home on his handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW) 19 days ago; may have needed that first start for 136 days but needs to take step forward on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Shaped encouragingly on his third start but disappointed on his handicap debut; turf debut

runners.name: Lordsbridge Bay
runners.spotlight: Progressed with each start in novice/maiden company but weakened into seventh on his handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) 35 days ago; may be suited by this drop back in trip on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Weakened into seventh on his handicap debut at Lingfield; drops back to 7f on turf debut

runners.name: Poetic Grace
runners.spotlight: Well held in three starts as a two but she's shown more in two starts in handicaps with blinkers added this year; fourth of ten at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) two weeks ago and has each-way claims back on turf.
runners.comment: Has shown more since handicapping with blinkers on; each-way claims back on turf

runners.name: Blueaway
runners.spotlight: 0-4 for Rod Milman and beaten 14l when last of six on her handicap debut at Yarmouth (5f, good) three weeks ago; hood, which has been worn on all five starts, is removed and she now steps up in trip.
runners.comment: Last of six on her stable debut at Yarmouth (5f); up in trip with the hood removed

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: 0-10; made an encouraging stable debut at Leicester (7f, good) last month, finishing fourth of 17, but wasn't able to build on that at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) 28 days ago; drops back in trip with a place chance.
runners.comment: Encouraging yard debut at Leicester (7f) but failed to build on that over 1m at Yarmouth

runners.name: Petes Diamond
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has yet to make the frame; well beaten at Leicester (7f, good) last month and midfield at Nottingham (6f, good; one place behind stablemate Power Of Chora) 23 days ago; steps back in trip; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-8; one place behind stablemate Power Of Chora when midfield at Nottingham (6f) last time

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but placed in both starts since being gelded, the most recent coming at Kempton (7f, AW; first-time cheekpieces) 19 days ago behind a rapid improver who has won again since; headgear is retained but he's been well beaten in his two starts on turf.
runners.comment: Placed in both starts since being gelded but well beaten in his two starts on turf

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in novice company and plugged on for sixth on her handicap debut at Nottingham (6f, good; first-time tongue-tie) 23 days ago, shaping as if this step back up in trip will suit; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Plugged on for sixth on her handicap debut at Nottingham over 6f; steps back up in trip

runners.name: Bear Steps
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two starts over 7f last year and beaten 8l when sixth of eight in a novice at Southwell (7f, AW) 42 days ago; makes her turf/handicap debut and may yet do better; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts in novice/maiden company; makes her turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Demetris Mouflon
runners.spotlight: Favourite when last of five on handicap debut at Chepstow (1m, soft) in September and was down the field at Southwell (6f, AW) next time; off 238 days since and has blinkers added for her reappearance.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on her h'cap debut and well held next time; blinkers on for reappearance

runners.name: Back To Black
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly but she beat only one rival home in three maiden events (5f/6f) for William Haggas last year; has since changed stables (same owner) and steps up in trip for her handicap debut; market should be informative.
runners.comment: Disappointing in three starts for William Haggas; steps up in trip for yard/handicap debut

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier) (Div I)

runners.name: Reidh
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Richard Fahey in the summer of 2024 but lost his way; sold for 10,000gns to join new yard in October; gambled on but well held on stable debut at Thirsk (7f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (now removed); may be worth another chance off sliding mark.
runners.comment: Gambled on but well held on stable debut at Thirsk (7f, good); may be worth another chance
runners.owner: Jack Nunn, Tom Korolkiewicz And Partner

runners.name: English Lady
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f Polytrack winner; has had only a couple of goes on turf and may be open to improvement.
runners.comment: Has had a couple of only goes on turf and may be open to improvement.

runners.name: Francisco
runners.spotlight: Yet to score on turf but last month he recorded his second AW win, at this track (7f); holds claims if he can reproduce his best back on turf.
runners.comment: Yet to score on turf but last month he recorded his second AW win, at this track (7f).

runners.name: Play Me
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who got off the mark at Epsom (8.5f, good) last August and has made the frame in all five starts since, latest when third of 12 here (1m, AW) last month following six-month break; should strip fitter and be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won at Epsom last August and has made the frame in all five starts since.
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Call Glory
runners.spotlight: Won twice on Southwell AW in January and usually thereabouts since but he is 1-19 on turf; should go close but his win record is off-putting.
runners.comment: Won twice on Southwell AW in January; usually thereabouts since but he is 1-19 on turf

runners.name: Pitney
runners.spotlight: Both wins when trained by Hugo Palmer; now finding it difficult to get his head in front and has dropped to a career-low mark; may get in the firing line but doesn't appeal for win purposes.
runners.comment: Finding it difficult to get his head in front and has dropped to a career-low mark.

runners.name: Bated Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but has gone close on several occasions; returns from a seven-month break and probably best watched.
runners.comment: 0-18 but has gone close on several occasions; returns from a seven-month break.

runners.name: Tronido
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who won at Sandown last August and followed up by 6l at Epsom (both on soft) the following month off this mark; below that form this year though.
runners.comment: Won twice last term but below that form this year.

runners.name: King Of War
runners.spotlight: Has done all his winning at Chepstow and Brighton; absent since November and probably best watched for now.
runners.comment: Has done all his winning at Chepstow and Brighton.

runners.name: Arnaz
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when trained by Ed Walker in May 2024; down to a career-low mark but hasn't threatened on his recent starts on AW.
runners.comment: Down to a career-low mark but hasn't threatened on his recent starts on AW.

runners.name: Lunanova
runners.spotlight: Won three times over 1m at Brighton last summer but has lost her way; finished last on her two starts this term, for new yard.
runners.comment: Won three times over 1m at Brighton last summer but has lost her way.

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier) (Div II)

runners.name: My Mate Kev
runners.spotlight: Last win was at Newmarket in May 2025 off 11lb higher; only sixth of eight at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago when 6-4 favourite; steps back up in trip and is so well handicapped on old form that he must be considered.
runners.comment: Last win was at Newmarket in May 2025; 11lb lower today and has to be considered.

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the AW at Chelmsford in January over 7f; outpaced on his return to turf here (5f, good to firm) ten days ago until staying on well for fourth; back to a more suitable distance and not out of this.
runners.comment: Outpaced on his return to turf here (5f) ten days ago until staying on well for fourth.

runners.name: Beau Jardine
runners.spotlight: All three wins on turf came on soft/heavy; it's been a while since his last success but he may be able to exploit this plummeting mark when he gets his conditions.
runners.comment: All three wins on turf came on soft/heavy; it's been a while since his last success.

runners.name: Jackson Street
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on AW; returns from a break and best watched unless there is a market move.
runners.comment: Returns from a break and best watched unless there is a market move.

runners.name: Rusheen Boy
runners.spotlight: Has won on turf but four of his five wins have been on AW; it may be best to wait until he returns to AW.
runners.comment: Has won on turf but four of his five wins have been on AW.

runners.name: Twitch
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2024 but went close here (7f, AW) last month; this race may be more competitive than he is used to but he's not dismissed.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2024 but went close here last month; not dismissed.
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Groundsman
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with a front-running win at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in September, his final start for Richard Fahey; three fair efforts for his new yard but needs to reproduce his best on turf following a five-month break.
runners.comment: Three fair efforts for his new yard but needs to reproduce his best on turf.

runners.name: Giant
runners.spotlight: All three wins on AW; decent third at Southwell (1m, AW) last month but unable to back that up returning to turf when 14th of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm); seems better on AW.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell last month but unable to back that up returning to turf at Doncaster.

runners.name: Regal Guest
runners.spotlight: Promising third at Kempton (7f, AW) last March on his second start but has beaten only one rival in three handicaps since; very lightly raced and first-time cheekpieces are now applied; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Third at Kempton last March but has beaten only one rival in three handicaps since.

runners.name: The Organiser
runners.spotlight: His fourth win (Brighton last September) temporarily halted a downward trajectory but he hasn't been placed since; best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Won at Brighton last September but he hasn't been placed since.

runners.name: Havanita
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from six starts; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from six starts; hard to fancy.
runners.owner: Mrs R Lyon & P R Chamings

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Cameley Days
runners.spotlight: Back to form returning to the minimum trip when winning for the third time, at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; up 8lb but may have more in her locker.
runners.comment: Won for the third time, at Windsor 12 days ago.

runners.name: Desdemona
runners.spotlight: Has won over C&D but has been kept busy on AW of late, winning on the AW track here and at Chelmsford earlier in the year; interesting if she can reproduce her form back on turf.
runners.comment: Has won over C&D, and also earlier this year on the AW tracks here and at Chelmsford.

runners.name: Teardrops
runners.spotlight: Has been campaigned exclusively on AW where he had three wins over 6f at Newcastle for Richard Spencer; sold for 5,000gns to join new trainer last month and is a fascinating contender back in trip on turf debut for shrewd yard.
runners.comment: Has been campaigned exclusively on AW where he has three wins over 6f at Newcastle.

runners.name: Harry Brown
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a career-low mark to land his first win on turf over C&D (good to firm) ten days ago; up 4lb but will need luck in running to weave through.
runners.comment: Took advantage of a career-low mark to land his first win on turf over C&D ten days ago.
runners.owner: Peter Crate And Gallagher Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Has seven wins on turf and is edging closer to another judged on his recent seconds at Bath and Nottingham; should go close.
runners.comment: Has seven wins on turf and is edging closer to another judged on two recent seconds.

runners.name: Silky Robin
runners.spotlight: Won four races on turf last year and resumes after an unsuccessful stint on the AW, 2lb lower than when last successful; fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Won four races on turf last year and resumes after an unsuccessful stint on the AW.

runners.name: Cabeza De Llave
runners.spotlight: Has six wins to his name but the last one came in October 2024; second on his return to turf at Windsor (5f, good) and should run well.
runners.comment: Second on his return to turf at Windsor and should run well.

runners.name: Call Time
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft ground; probably best to leave him alone until he gets his conditions.
runners.comment: Both wins on soft ground; probably best to leave him alone until he gets his conditions.
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Addictive
runners.spotlight: Maiden who finished last of 11 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 15 days ago on his stable debut on return from a lengthy layoff; best watched.
runners.comment: Maiden who finished last at Windsor 15 days ago on her return from a lengthy layoff.

runners.name: Fletcher's Flight
runners.spotlight: All four wins when trained by Philip McBride, the last of which was in September 2024; sold for 6,000gns to join new yard in February; unseated when rearing at the start on stable debut; first-time cheekpieces go on and he's best watched.
runners.comment: Unseated when rearing at start on stable debut; first-time cheekpieces go on; best watched

runners.name: Faustus
runners.spotlight: Has won five times on turf but is not the force of old; has dropped a career-low mark but this race looks too competitive.
runners.comment: Five turf wins but not the force of old; career-low mark but this looks too competitive.

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Alaskan Bear
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt ran a fine race on debut to chase home the Coventry Stakes-bound Ballydoyle colt Confucius at Naas (6f, good) 10 days ago; not sure whether this sharper test will suit but leading form claims.
runners.comment: Fine effort behind Ballydoyle hotpot on debut at Naas, drops to 5f, leading form claims

runners.name: Away And Gone
runners.spotlight: Ardad filly; price rose from E17,000 as a foal to 40,000GBP as a 2yo; half-sister to 1m winners Eminent Hipster (RPR 83) and Silk Bird (74); dam 1m winner (77); in good hands and market check advised.
runners.comment: Ardad filly, 40,000GBP 2yo, dam 1m winner, in good hands, market check advised

runners.name: Belle Of The Ball
runners.spotlight: Mohaather filly showed plenty of early speed before fading into a 2.75l fifth in the closing stages on debut at the Curragh (6f, good); the drop back to 5f looks a plus on that evidence; pedigree suggests easier conditions should be fine; leading claims.
runners.comment: Early dash before fading over 6f on debut, drop in trip a plus, leading form claims
runners.owner: OMEGA Racing & Belle Morris & Russell B Jones Jr

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
