runners.spotlight: Showcasing colt sent off at 18-1 on debut at the Curragh (5f, good) and ran a decent race to finish a 5.75l third; the fourth from that contest finished a clear second in a Navan maiden on Saturday; likely to improve and he's a player.
runners.comment: Showcasing colt, promise on debut at the Curragh, form boosted, should improve, player

runners.name: Arrowstock
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; E17,000 yearling; dam 6f/7f winner (inc 2yo; RPR 90), out of maiden half-sister to 6f Listed winner Inyordreams and Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Fire Eyes, is granddaughter of 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Wunders Dream; plenty of appeal on paper.
runners.comment: E17,000 Cotai Glory filly, dam 2yo winner, makes plenty of appeal on paper

runners.name: Tatianna
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly; E20,000 foal; unsold for 9,000gns as a yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Qaroun (useful 7f/1m) and Puerto De Vega (6f/7f), out of 5f winner; yard had 2yo maiden winner on Saturday; market can guide.
runners.comment: Kameko filly, dam unraced, yard had 2yo maiden winner on Saturday, market can guide

runners.name: The Drambling Man
runners.spotlight: Soldier's Call gelding; E7,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Specialmente (French/HK 5f-7f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 93), Mayaada (7f 2yo; 78) and Lady Maeve (Canadian 7f-8.5f turf/AW); dam unplaced 6f-1m2f (65); best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: E7,000 Soldier's Call gelding, half-brother to two 2yo winners, watch unless market speaks

runners.name: Shafters Legacy
runners.spotlight: Invincible Spirit filly; E20,000 Y; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Calming Influence (UAE 1m Group 2) and Steel Light (Canadian 6f Grade 2), out of 7f winner; respected source; check market.
runners.comment: E20,000 Invincible Spirit filly, dam unraced, respected source, check the market

runners.name: Stellar Path
runners.spotlight: Naval Crown filly; dam Italian 9.5f 2yo winner, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Isaac Shelby, out of unraced half-sister to US 1m6f Grade 2 winner Ramazutti; first juvenile runner for yard this year; best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Naval Crown filly, dam Italian 9.5f 2yo winner, best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Jewel In A Crowd
runners.spotlight: Hello Youmzain colt; E90,000 Y; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Waldersee (French/German 6.5-7f inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 105) and Sunshinefled (French 7f); dam French Listed-placed 4f-1m winner (inc 2yo; 98); market check advised.
runners.comment: E90,000 Hello Youmzain colt, half-brother to 2yo winner, market check advised
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs Dolores Gleeson

runners.name: Sirocco Sands
runners.spotlight: Persian Force colt; E65,000 Y; second foal; half-brother to yard's 6f AW winner Allsortz (RPR 85); dam French 10.7f winner (inc AW), half-sister to winners Subway Dancer (1m3f Group 2) and Secret Advisor (1m6f Group 3); market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: E65,000 Persian Force colt, dam French 10.7f winner, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Queenofthewild
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 7-1 on debut over C&D (soft) but was outpaced and never managed to land a blow; will need to improve plenty on that to make an impact here.
runners.comment: Outpaced and never landed a blow on debut over C&D, improvement needed

runners.name: Mr Montauk
runners.spotlight: With a top yard but has failed to fire in a pair of 5f maidens at Naas; beaten 12.5l when last of seven there on latest; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Failed to fire in two 5f maidens at Naas, last of seven latest, others make more appeal

races.race_name: Follow Us On Social Media Handicap

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; won 6f Dundalk maiden on penultimate, making all; close third behind Amiata and Bad Boy Rizz in Naas handicap latest (6f, good); mark left alone so should be thereabouts again but sole start on easy ground was poor so probably wouldn't want to see much rain.
runners.comment: Close third behind Amiata and Bad Boy Rizz latest, same mark, forecast rain a worry

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Navan maiden winner last September (6f, good); no impact in listed company at Dundalk and Newmarket next twice; back on song with 1.25l third of 14 in premier handicap at Naas latest (6f, good); just 1lb higher now; forecast rain a concern.
runners.comment: Navan maiden winner last season, back to form at Naas latest, soft would be a query

runners.name: Bad Boy Rizz
runners.spotlight: Yard in red hot form; excellent effort when 1.5l behind Amiata on seasonal return at Naas (6f, good); maiden win came over 5f on soft so forecast rain a plus and is 6lb better off with Amiata now, who had a fitness edge last time; leading claims.
runners.comment: 6lb swing with Amiata for 1.5l defeat on latest, strip fitter now, soft fine, big chance

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Tipperary maiden winner last season (5f, yielding); not far behind a few of these when fifth at Naas on penultimate (6f, good); held by stablemate Treasure Royal in 6f premier handicap back there on latest; drop to 5f and easier ground a plus; not discounted.
runners.comment: Tipperary maiden winner, not far behind a few of these on penultimate, rain a plus

runners.name: Tornado Kiss
runners.spotlight: Beat Bad Boy Rizz by 0.5l when winning a C&D maiden on his third start last season; came up short in listed heats at Dundalk last October and here on seasonal return in two starts since; could go well on handicap debut.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, no joy in listed company last twice, could go well on h'cap debut

runners.name: Rosie Frith
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden (good to firm) last July; went close in auction series race (6f, yld-sft) here in September; scored in 5f Dundalk handicap off 13lb lower in October; out of depth in listed race here on return (5.5f, soft); may be on tough mark.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, out of depth in listed company latest, may be on tough mark

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: 5f Navan maiden winner (yielding) gained first handicap win at Naas on latest (6f, good), beating Bad Boy Rizz by 1.5l; raised 7lb and drops back in trip; handles softer ground and could be more to come; yard amongst the winners; player.
runners.comment: Raised 7lb for 6f win at Naas latest; drops in trip, handles soft, yard in form; chance

runners.name: Bay Of Supremacy
runners.spotlight: 2-7 on turf; has returned in fine form this season; close third at Naas (5f, soft); went two places better at Bellewstown (5f, soft) on latest; raised 7lb and this is harder but the forecast rain is a big plus for him; dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: 2-7, raised 7lb for win on latest, this harder but rain a big plus, dangerous to dismiss
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Soulful Diva
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; won C&D maiden (good) last August; outclassed in Longchamp Group 3 later that month; fourth in conditions race at Dundalk when last seen in September; worth a market check on seasonal return and handicap debut.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner on good, worth market check on seasonal return/h'cap debut

runners.name: Fox In Flight
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; no impact in first two maidens on turf; off the mark in 5f Dundalk maiden in December; solid third in conditions race (5f) when last seen later that month; makes handicap debut on seasonal return; McAteer prefers stablemate.
runners.comment: 5f Dundalk maiden winner, off since December, handicap debut, McAteer deserts

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Won a Down Royal handicap (5f, yielding) last September off 6lb lower; yet to really fire this term and is 3lb wrong here; rider's claim negates that but others still preferred.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower last year, yet to fire this term, 3lb wrong, others preferred

races.race_name: Buy Tickets Online At www.corkracecourse.ie Handicap

runners.name: Jordan Breeze
runners.spotlight: Fine run on seasonal return at Dundalk and again when a 1l second of 15 at Bellewstown (5f, soft) on latest; in the mix again off the same mark.
runners.comment: Fine run on return and again when a close second latest, same mark, in the mix again

runners.name: Steel Magnolia
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb higher at Bellewstown (5f, soft) last October; close fourth back there on seasonal return in April; player again off the same mark with forecast rain in her favour.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb higher last October, fine run on seasonal return, player off same mark

runners.name: An Laochmor
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW earlier in the year and continued in the same vein on return to turf with a solid 2.25l second of 16 at Navan last month; ran fourth in this last season off 6lb lower; player.
runners.comment: In form on AW, good run on return to turf at Navan, fourth in this last year off 6lb lower

runners.name: Hero Of The Hour
runners.spotlight: Won seven races for Tom McCourt, including three on turf; good run when last seen at Dundalk in March; capable off this mark but forecast rain a concern; new yard.
runners.comment: 3-21 on turf, new yard, good AW run when last seen, forecast rain a concern

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Seven race maiden whose best efforts have come on the AW; not a bad effort over 1m here in April but didn't back it up at the Curragh (7f, good) latest; eased 2lb and tries 5f for the first time; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-7, best efforts on AW, well beaten over 7f latest, first try at 5f, others preferred

runners.name: Art Of Unity
runners.spotlight: Veteran has shaped well in two of his three runs this season; has slipped to last winning mark and forecast rain is in his favour; has run well over C&D before; could go well.
runners.comment: Veteran, two good runs this season, on last winning mark, has C&D form, rain a plus

runners.name: Ballysax Lil' Mick
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb higher at Tipperary last season (5f, good); failed to beat a rival when last seen at Dundalk in January; record when fresh suggests he might need this run.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb higher last season, poor at Dundalk when last seen in Jan', might need this

runners.name: Only Spoofing
runners.spotlight: Hardy veteran has won fourteen races on turf, all on good and good to firm ground, but handles an ease; is 15lb lower than when winning at Musselburgh last year and didn't run badly at Bellewstown (5f, soft) on latest; could go well.
runners.comment: Hardy veteran, not a bad run at Bellewstown latest, on dangerous mark, could go well

runners.name: Snowballs
runners.spotlight: Maiden is 0-5 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; beaten 4l when seventh of 16 in first-time blinkers (retained) at Naas on latest; eased 3lb; needs more and soft ground would be a query.
runners.comment: 0-5 on turf, eased 3lb for 4l defeat latest, soft would be a query, more needed

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: 0-16 on turf; has run well in defeat on good and soft ground; ran okay from out of the handicap over 5f on seasonal return at Naas (5f, good) when 0.5l behind Art Of Unity; has gone well over C&D; 11-2 when midfield at Navan (6f, yielding) 3 days ago, slightly hampered; drop back in trip a plus; top rider booked.
runners.comment: 0-16 on turf but has run well in defeat, has gone well over C&D before, place shout

runners.name: Jazzit
runners.spotlight: Ten race maiden is 0-2 on turf; shaped with a bit of promise at Navan (6f, yielding) three days ago and on that evidence, this sharper test could suit; one to consider.
runners.comment: Maiden, 0-2 on turf, shaped like this sharper test might suit at Navan latest, considered

runners.name: Lady Landhort
runners.spotlight: 3yo's sole success came in a 6f Dundalk handicap in March off 49; came up short off revised mark twice since; unraced on softer than good on turf; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: AW win off 3lb lower in March, beaten off revised mark since, others appeal more
runners.owner: T J Flanagan & Ronan Macken & Shane McSweeney & S O'Malley

runners.name: Windsor Lily
runners.spotlight: 3yo; standout effort a 2.25l fifth in a 6f Dundalk maiden in February; no impact in first handicap over 7f on soft at Gowran; dam a 5f winner so this trip could suit but a bit to prove for now.
runners.comment: Best run over 6f in Dundalk maiden, no impact over 7f on h'cap debut, a bit to prove
runners.owner: Shane R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Kilmood Susan
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a C&D maiden (5f good) last June for Daniel Murphy; yet to fire since joining this stable but drops in grade now and is ground versatile; can't discount.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, poor this season but drops in grade, ground versatile, can't discount

runners.name: Spirit Above
runners.spotlight: 3yo showed no signs of winning potential in maidens or on handicap debut at Dundalk (6f) latest; can only be watched down in trip in first turf handicap.
runners.comment: No sign yet of winning potential, can only be watched in first turf handicap

runners.name: Jered Maddox
runners.spotlight: Veteran is prolific on the AW but has a record of 1-28 on turf with the win coming in 2019; failed to beat a rival at the Curragh on latest (6f, good); same mark here.
runners.comment: 1-28 on turf, failed to beat a rival at the Curragh latest, same mark here

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: 3yo; modest form in good ground turf maidens last year; shaped well at Dundalk on penultimate (6f); weakened upped to 7f back there when last seen in January; this trip more suitable but probably best watched in first turf handicap.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Dundalk on penultimate, off since January, probably best watched

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Star Glory
runners.spotlight: Offered something to build on with a never nearer 5.25l sixth of 12 on debut at the Curragh (6f, good) after getting upset in the stalls; should improve for that and could go well if acting on likely softer ground.
runners.comment: Never nearer sixth on debut, should improve, go well if acts on likely softer ground

runners.name: Alrashdeyah
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly; E27,000 Y; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to Listed winners Spark Plug (1m2f/1m4f), Slight Advantage (1m7f) and Pearl Of Africa (1m), out of smart 7f-1m2f winner in South Africa; yard in form; market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: E27,000 Churchill filly, dam unraced, yard in form, market can guide on debut

runners.name: Hanney Boy
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt sent off at 28-1 on debut here (5f, soft) but outran those odds to finish a staying on second behind Edward Thatch; the third from that race Matriarchal won at Navan on Saturday; big chance upped to 6f.
runners.comment: 28-1 but ran a fine race on debut here over 5f, form boosted, 6f to suit, big chance

runners.name: Shesthedevil
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; E3,500 Y; second foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 83), sister to winners Mr Rumbalicious (2m Flat/2m1f hurdle) and Eye For An Eye (Australian 8.5f); hooded for debut and likely best watched.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly; dam 7f winner, hooded for debut, likely best watched

runners.name: Chiquitita Bee
runners.spotlight: State Of Rest filly; E12,000 Y; second foal; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 82), half-sister to Italian 1m7f jumps winner Teddy's Profit, out of French 1m2f winning half-sister to 5f/5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Signora Cabello; market check advised.
runners.comment: E12,000 State Of Rest filly, dam runner up 6f (2yo), market check advised

runners.name: Maggie Walker
runners.spotlight: King of Change filly; E6,500 Y; third foal; dam unplaced 1m2f/11.6f (RPR 57), sister to Australian 1m4f Group 2 winner Saracen Knight, half-sister to Australian 1m3f Group 2 winner Fawkner Park; bred to need further in time.
runners.comment: King of Change filly, dam related to 1m3f-1m4f Gr'2 winners, may need further in time

runners.name: I Ready
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army gelding; E10,000 Y; second foal; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 74), half-sister to winners Insignia Of Rank (1m Listed) and Mayleaf Shine (useful 5f inc 2yo), out of 5f 2yo winner; speedily bred sort; check market.
runners.comment: Speedily-bred Invincible Army gelding, worth checking in the market

runners.name: Sophia Royale
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and failed to land a blow over C&D on debut (soft); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 66-1 and failed to make an impact over C&D on debut, others appeal more

runners.name: Portaliffe Phil
runners.spotlight: Awtaad colt; E35,000 yearling; brother to German 9.5f-1m7f winner Tiki Fire, half-brother to winners Domfront (French/Qatari 1m-1m3f inc 2yo; RPR 94) and Dear Power (6f AW 2yo; 84); dam German 1m/9.5f winner; market can guide.
runners.comment: E35,000 Awtaad colt, half-brother to two 2yo winners, market can guide

runners.name: Cromane
runners.spotlight: Territories filly; 42,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Alkaamel (7f-8.6f inc AW/dirt; RPR 106) and Give And Take (7.6f 2yo/1m2f Group 3; 101); dam unraced sister to 1m2f-2m4f Group 1 winner Fame And Glory; nicely bred; check the market.
runners.comment: 42,000gns Territories filly, dam half-sister to classy Fame And Glory, check market

runners.name: Desert Alchemist
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt; 42,000gns yearling; dam twice-raced half-sister to 1m6f Group 2 winner Carmers, out of 1m-1m2f winning half-sister to Group 1 winners Lord Shanakill (1m), Forever Together (1m4f) and Together Forever (1m 2yo); nicely bred but may need further in time.
runners.comment: 42,000gns Ghaiyyath colt, dam half-sister to talented stayer Carmers, may need further

runners.name: Switching Sides
runners.spotlight: Kodiac colt bought back for E52,000 as a yearling; dam placed 1m3f/1m4f (RPR 76), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Besharah; needs checking in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Kodiac colt, dam half-sister to 2yo 6f Group 2 winner, needs checking in the market

runners.name: Granite Mountain
runners.spotlight: 33-1 on debut at the Curragh (5f, heavy) and failed to beat a rival; may need more time.
runners.comment: 33-1 and failed to beat a rival on debut at the Curragh, may need more time

runners.name: Seven Nation Army
runners.spotlight: Top yard but hasn't shown enough in maidens at Roscommon and Navan to be of interest here; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Top yard but down the field in both previous starts, others make more appeal

races.race_name: Cork Racecourse Maiden

runners.name: Al Haarith
runners.spotlight: 475,000gns Lope De Vega colt; smart level of form at two in three runs at the Curragh, notably only losing second late on in 5-runner Group 2 Beresford Stakes (1m, yielding-to-soft) won easily by Hawk Mountain; back in maiden company on seasonal debut and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Smart juvenile form at 7f/1m makes him the one to beat on seasonal return

runners.name: The Piper's Call
runners.spotlight: Decent level of form in two runs last summer at 6f (good), second of 13 behind subsequent Group 3 runner-up Flushing Meadows at the Curragh and (odds-on) not quite as good form but decent nonetheless when third at Naas; gelded since and upped in trip on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Two good runs last summer at 6f on good ground; upped in trip on seasonal return

runners.name: Kilmac Air
runners.spotlight: Recent fourth here (6f) a step in the right direction but a lot more likely required here.
runners.comment: Recent fourth here (6f) a step in the right direction but a lot more likely required here
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs M V Magnier

runners.name: Into Tomorrow
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly; first foal; dam 1m AW winner (RPR 61), closely related to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Hatta Fort, half-sister to Group 3 winner Blue Bayou (7f 2yo); yard's more likely of two newcomers, worth a market check.
runners.comment: St Mark's Basilica filly; yard's more likely of two newcomers, worth a market check

runners.name: Shesharna
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier half-sister to winners Shelir (7f/1m inc Listed; RPR 115), Shekhem (Group-placed 7f 2yo; 107), Shelaka (7.4f 2yo; 82) and Shelannga (7f; 80); dam placed 7f 2yo (80), sister to 1m2f and 1m7f Group 3 winner Shemima; debutante worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-sister to several decent sorts; debutante worth a market check
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Sioux The Boss
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly cost 70,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Measure Of Time (French 1m1f-1m6f inc 2yo/Listed; RPR 102) and Seattle Sound (12.3f; 83); dam unraced sister to 1m2f/1m4f Listed Flat winner and smart jumper Stage Affair; debutante worth a market check.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly cost 70,000euros as a yearling; worth a market check on debut
runners.owner: John G Hennessy & C Acheson & Mrs John Harrington

runners.name: Airborne Ranger
runners.spotlight: Starman colt cost 21,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to 5f-7f winner Hurricane Ivor (inc 2yo/Group 3; RPR 113); unraced dam closely related to 5f/6f winner Minley; debutant best watched unless market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Debutant best watched unless market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Highwayman
runners.spotlight: Persian King half-brother to French 1m3f winner Swann (RPR 95); dam once-raced half-sister to winners Saliko (1m2f), Sayed (1m-1m4f) and Salyazi (1m3f), out of 1m winning sister to 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Slickly; yard second-choice, worth a look for future reference.
runners.comment: Newcomer yard second-choice, worth a look for future reference

runners.name: Kula Bula Boyo
runners.spotlight: Arizona half-brother to German 6f/6.5f winner Maid Of Dragon; dam German 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Yourtimeisnow; debutant likely all the better for the experience.
runners.comment: Debutant likely all the better for the experience

runners.name: Mountain Memory
runners.spotlight: Nando Parrado half-brother to Italian 6f/7.5f winner Cat The Black; dam 6f winner (RPR 53), half-sister to useful 7f-1m1f winner Ronald R; yard in terrific form, worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Nando Parrado gelding; yard in terrific form, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Pacioli
runners.spotlight: 28,000gns yearling; Due Diligence gelding is the second foal out of 7f AW 2yo winner; 50-1, wasn't discredited on debut here early in the month, staying on late when fifth of 15; improvement expected now upped in trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut here recently; improvement expected now upped in trip

runners.name: Ocean Echo
runners.spotlight: No threat in maidens at Gowran and here; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: No threat in maidens at Gowran and here; can only be watched for now
runners.owner: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke

runners.name: Hydream
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners inc Vadream (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 111), Humam (6f/7f inc 2yo/AW; 99) and Midream (6f; 94); dam maiden (69) half-sister to Australian 1m5f Group 2 winner Swashbuckling; never sighted after slow start on recent Naas debut.
runners.comment: Never sighted after slow start on recent Naas debut

runners.name: Miss Mercier
runners.spotlight: Three runs last year for John Burke amounted to little; new yard but can only be watched.
runners.comment: Three runs last year for John Burke amounted to little; new yard but can only be watched

runners.name: Dc Diva
runners.spotlight: Midfield finish on debut in Dundalk maiden in March 2024 and has been absent since.
runners.comment: Midfield finish on debut in Dundalk maiden in March 2024 and has been absent since

races.race_name: Blackwater Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Gleneagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on debut at Dundalk in 2024; 0-13 since but ran well in top handicaps last season, including fifth in Irish Lincoln, third in Victoria Cup at Ascot and second in Ahonoora at Galway; C&D run last month was a solid run and dropped 2lb since.
runners.comment: Long losing run but C&D run last month was a solid effort and dropped 2lb since

runners.name: Send Harry
runners.spotlight: Model of consistency on turf last summer, when his form included two Curragh placings over 6f; subsequently won over today's trip on Dundalk AW; recent Curragh comeback run should have brought him on so worth considering.
runners.comment: Recent Curragh comeback run should have brought him on so worth considering

runners.name: Goal Exceeded
runners.spotlight: Two wins include over this trip at the Curragh a year ago off 73; fine comeback run at Leopardstown but race may have come too soon when only midfield at the Curragh four days later; short break since and remains nicely handicapped, with in-form rider's claim an added bonus.
runners.comment: Remains nicely handicapped, with in-form rider's claim an added bonus
runners.owner: Ms H McGuinness/Martin W White/Declan Sheridan/Breen White

runners.name: L L Koulsty
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo won 6f maiden here last September (yielding-to-soft) and has acquitted himself creditably in two handicap runs this season, latest when not quite getting home (rider dropped whip) over 1m in finishing fourth here early this month; down 2lb but remains 3lb out of handicap proper; don't rule out with drop to 7f to suit.
runners.comment: Ran well here latest over 1m; 3lb wrong but could go close now dropped in trip

runners.name: Mallavelly
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last year; went up an aggregate 11lb for wins at Leopardstown and Tipperary and ended season with fine third in premier handicap at former venue; out of her depth on comeback run in Leopardstown Listed nine days ago, considered back at her level here.
runners.comment: Out of her depth on comeback run nine days ago, considered back at her level here

runners.name: Brewing
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been gained on AW; in good form at Dundalk last winter, soft ground probably wasn't ideal on turf comeback (slowly away) over C&D early this month and fair bit to find with Gleneagle Bay on that run.
runners.comment: AW winner; soft ground probably wasn't ideal on turf comeback over C&D early this month

runners.name: Smoke Them Out
runners.spotlight: Notched a third career win when making all on seasonal return over 6f at the Curragh in March (heavy) but just fair efforts twice since at same venue; yet to win over this far and best form on easier ground so would welcome rain.
runners.comment: Yet to win over this far and best form on easier ground so would welcome rain

runners.name: Usuario Amigo
runners.spotlight: 7f Brighton maiden win in September 2024; yet to score for this yard, best effort when third in a rated race at Limerick last summer (6.5f, good), most recent run at Naas 10 days ago not without promise and return to 7f could suit.
runners.comment: Run at Naas 10 days ago not without promise and return to 7f could suit

runners.name: Dragon Of Malta
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner at 7f/1m, last three wins coming at Listowel on soft/heavy; has tended to need his comeback run so probably best watched here on ground on the quick side.
runners.comment: Tended to need his comeback run so probably best watched here on ground on the quick side

races.race_name: Mallow Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Carmel's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Two promising efforts last year in maidens at the Curragh and Bellewstown and posted a much higher RPR on seasonal return when under 9l off winner in Listed Noblesse Stakes over C&D last month (soft-to-heavy); interesting to see if she can run to her 86 rating here back in maiden company.
runners.comment: C&D Listed fifth last month makes her a leading player back in maiden company

runners.name: Benaki
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Charlus (Listed-placed 9.5f/1m2f inc 2yo/AW, RPR 96; 2m hurdle), Kosciuszko (1m-1m2f inc 2yo/Spain; 94) and Aquileo (Australian 1m-11.5f); dam French Group-placed 10.5f winner (102); promising Limerick debut last month over this trip in chasing home a ready odds-on winner, well clear of the remainder; could improve enough to feature.
runners.comment: Recent debut Limerick second could well improve enough to feature

runners.name: Sangreal
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly a 270,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Roi De France (Listed-placed 1m; RPR 112) and Vaguely Royal (1m4f Flat, 100); dam 1m/8.5f winner (inc AW 2yo/US turf stakes; 97); promising debut run in 1m2f Leopardstown fillies maiden last month when chasing home a clear winner over 1m2f (Camelot Queen 1l away in third); should appreciate longer trip (withdrawn from Navan Listed on Saturday).
runners.comment: Leopardstown debut second expected to improve for this longer trip; favourite's chance.

runners.name: I Hope You Dance
runners.spotlight: Fair form in a couple of juvenile runs, taking a step forward on last month's return at Dundalk when second in 1m4f maiden, having had every chance in straight (winner has run well in handicap since); more likely required back on turf but stamina proven and place claims.
runners.comment: Recent Dundalk second; more likely required but stamina proven and place claims

runners.name: Camelot Queen
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly showed improvement on last month's seasonal return in 1m2f Leopardstown fillies maiden, forcing much of pace when third to a smart type (Sangreal a staying-on second); not sure this step up in trip will be in her favour (has Oaks entries) but seemingly the Ballydoyle first-choice.
runners.comment: Leopardstown comeback third encouraging; not sure this step up in trip will suit

runners.name: Werifesteria
runners.spotlight: War Of Will half-sister to winners Solar Halo (6f; RPR 89), In The Present (7f AW 2yo; 88), Landbiscuit (US 1m-1m1f turf/dirt/AW) and Yulong Storm (Australian 5.5f-6.5f); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (81), closely related to Derby winner Kris Kin; cheekpieces fitted for debut, market should guide.
runners.comment: War Of Will filly; cheekpieces fitted for debut, market should guide

runners.name: Invincible Destiny
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly bought cheaply as a yearling at 40,000euros; third foal; sister to French 7.7f winner Frankini; dam 5.5f (Group 3) and 6f (2yo) winner (RPR 111), closely related to 6.5f Group 1 winner Polydream; seemingly the second-choice of the Harrington pair of debutantes.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought Frankel filly; seemingly second-choice of the Harrington pair of debutantes

runners.name: Tickle The Cat
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon filly cost 36,000euros as a yearling; sister to winners Teddy Blue (1m6f Flat, RPR 83; useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Jumeirah King (2m hurdle); dam German 7f 2yo/1m Listed winner (102), half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Tickle Me Green; debutante best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Debutante best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Walls Cross
runners.spotlight: Waldgeist filly cost 9,000euros as a foal and 2,000euros as a yearling; third foal; half-sister to Italian 6.5f winner Boyka; dam placed 6f (RPR 68), half-sister to winners Samminder (Listed-placed 6f/7f) and Queen's Pearl (useful 6f); debutante likely best watched.
runners.comment: Cheaply bought Waldgeist filly; debutante likely best watched

runners.name: Cosmetic
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly is closely related to Group 1 winners Hydrangea, The United States and Hermosa; superbly-bred but has looked limited in three runs, most recently on seasonal return when beaten 11l in Dundalk AW fillies maiden last month; upped in trip here and cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Superbly-bred but has looked limited, beaten 11l in AW maiden on latest; upped in trip
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: De Vega Queen
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 38,000euros as a foal and 45,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Succeedandsurpass (US/Canadian Graded stakes-placed 1m turf/8.5f AW; RPR 110), Gaasy (KSA 1m2f) and Gordons Aura (2m hurdle); dam 1m 2yo/1m2f AW winner (89), out of Fillies' Mile winner; didn't count after slow start on last month's Gowran debut; more expected of now.
runners.comment: Didn't count after slow start on last month's Gowran debut; more expected of now

runners.name: Mare Crisium
runners.spotlight: Sea The Moon filly is half-sister to yard's Group-placed 1m winner Queen Of Thunder (RPR 109); dam 1m2f AW winner (91); didn't count, some late headway on recent C&D debut; expected to come on for that.
runners.comment: Didn't count, some late headway on recent C&D debut; expected to come on for that

runners.name: Gaoth Chuil
runners.spotlight: Decent NH mare, winning a bumper and three times over hurdles at up to 3m; recent chase form not quite at same level but useful; previous two Flat runs include decent fourth in 1m7f Leopardstown amateur maiden last summer; would probably appreciate a stiffer test.
runners.comment: Decent NH mare; would probably appreciate a stiffer test but place claims

races.race_name: Racing Again June 14th Handicap

runners.name: Magna Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: AW winner in Britain (9.5f); okay form for current yard on AW at end of last year but hasn't threatened in two turf runs this season (not clear run latest); more needed.
runners.comment: Hasn't threatened in two turf runs this season (not clear run latest); more needed

runners.name: Hosanna Power
runners.spotlight: No win since 1m2f Newbury win in May 2024; form this spring poor but handicapper quick to drop him and jockey booking looks positive.
runners.comment: Form this spring poor but handicapper quick to drop him and jockey booking looks positive

runners.name: Lady Luck
runners.spotlight: Moderate efforts in handicaps to date, mainly on the AW; declining mark but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Moderate efforts in handicaps to date, mainly on the AW; declining mark but hard to fancy

runners.name: Skib Gold
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; won at a monster price over 9.5f at Gowran last summer before good second over same C&D; two runs this term well below best .
runners.comment: Two runs this term well below best; reserve

runners.name: Khormor Syd
runners.spotlight: Maiden getting closer, good second last month at both Gowran (strong-finishing 1.25l off stablemate Starting Monday) and when caught late over 1m4f at Limerick; 3lb rise for those efforts okay, trip could be ideal.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice last month; 3lb higher but big player and trip could be ideal

runners.name: Red Veil
runners.spotlight: Placed in Gowran claimer last year for previous yard, form under both codes for current yard has been poor.
runners.comment: Placed in Gowran claimer last year; form under both codes for current yard has been poor

runners.name: Believeth
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo missed her 3yo season and not too much to take this year from two AW runs and most recently after racing up with pace in Leopardstown handicap (1m); upped markedly in trip now (dam won over 1m4f) and tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Didn't get home in Leopardstown handicap latest; upped markedly in trip and tongue-tied

runners.name: Highway Sixty One
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden for Andrew McNamara, whose best form is at 1m4f; hasn't been seen since October, best watched on yard debut.
runners.comment: Maiden's best form at 1m4f; hasn't been seen since October, best watched on yard debut

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Has returned with a couple of good runs, staying on fourth over a mile at Gowran and stayed 1m2f trip well when only beaten a neck at Sligo early this month, having led inside final furlong; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Two good runs this term; 3lb higher but definite chance if first-time cheekpieces work

runners.name: Not Simple
runners.spotlight: Won 7f handicap here last September (soft); failed to sustain progress afterwards but definite promise on last month's seasonal return at Gowran when never nearer fifth of 16 on similar ground; poor on only previous attempt at this trip but better ground could enable her to see it out.
runners.comment: Gowran comeback encouraging, though stamina doubt persists at this longer trip

runners.name: Fantasy Fara
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in trio of hot maidens last year and never sighted on handicap debut behind Starting Monday at Gowran last month; may need more time.
runners.comment: Never sighted on handicap debut behind Starting Monday at Gowran; may need more time

runners.name: Miss Crinshawn
runners.spotlight: First reserve; has returned this term with solid efforts at Dundalk and when not quite getting home over 1m4f at Limerick last month (1.25l off Khormor Syd); return to this shorter trip to suit and don't rule out if getting a run.
runners.comment: Return to this shorter trip to suit and don't rule out if getting a run; reserve

runners.name: Lauderdale King
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo; big prices and no threat in three maidens over shorter, most recently on yard/seasonal debut at Limerick last month; upped in trip for handicap debut and market should be interesting.
runners.comment: Nothing in maidens; upped in trip for handicap debut and market should be interesting

runners.name: Gold Dublooms
runners.spotlight: Third on handicap debut at Gowran (1m, good) a year ago but hasn't built on that effort; absent since January and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Gowran third a year hasn't been replicated; hard to fancy

runners.name: Starting Monday
runners.spotlight: Back to form for third career win when landing Gowran handicap over slightly shorter last month (soft) off career-low mark (beating strong-finishing stablemate Khormor Syd); wants it soft, may not confirm that form now.
runners.comment: Beat strong-finishing stablemate Khormor Syd at Gowran; may not confirm that form now

runners.name: Barretstown
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner whose sole turf win came in this race in 2024 (off 51); more miss than hit on AW over the winter but down to career-low mark now so don't rule out.
runners.comment: 2024 winner of this race; out of form on AW of late but down to career-low mark

runners.name: Seti
runners.spotlight: AW 2yo winner but not since, absent over a year since modest Navan effort at this trip in headgear combination; dropped 7lb since and down in grade here so don't dismiss.
runners.comment: Absent over a year but dropped 7lb since and down in grade here so don't dismiss

runners.name: Broadchurch
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo was never sighted on last month's return from year's absence on handicap debut at Gowran; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Never sighted on last month's return from year's absence at Gowran; cheekpieces now tried
runners.owner: J Moloney/Exors of the Late J C Fleming/Crampscastle B'stock

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; in the form of his life last June, notching three wins on the trot at 1m/1m1f following addition of visor/blinkers; yet to hit form in four attempts this term though.
runners.comment: In the form of his life last June but yet to hit form in four attempts this term; reserve

runners.name: Dress Dance
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three runs last year over shorter trips; not bred for this sort of trip and best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Struggled in three runs last year over shorter; not bred for this trip so best watched

races.race_name: Tynedale Agricultural Society Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Watchoutitscookie
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle and 2m7f chase winner (both good to soft); has struggled with consistency, but a leading player if reproducing his latest third of eight at Exeter last month.
runners.comment: Inconsistent, but a leading player if reproducing his latest third at Exeter last month.

runners.name: Dillarchie
runners.spotlight: Strong stayer who notched up three wins from February to April, though took a backward step last time over C&D off this mark; however, she's not out of this if repeating previous efforts with Oscar Palmer taking a valuable 3lb off her back.
runners.comment: Solid claims if recapturing the level of form she showed earlier in the year.

runners.name: Shighness
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for a couple of years and that was over fences, but she shaped encouragingly reverting to hurdles when third at Sedgefield (3m3f, soft) in February, albeit beaten nearly 19l, and has fallen back over fences since; however, she's on a handy mark and is hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: On a handy mark and back to some form on last hurdles start so hard to rule out.

runners.name: Jeteye
runners.spotlight: 2024 C&D winner; promising third when returning from a 15-month absence at Wetherby (3m, good) in March, in this headgear combination, proving he retains a good chunk of his ability; however, he'll need to quickly bounce back from a lesser effort at Perth last time, which tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Reappearance was encouraging but a lesser effort since tempers enthusiasm.

runners.name: My Boy Christy
runners.spotlight: Went close to shedding the maiden tag when runner-up over 2m4f here and backed that up with another solid second over C&D 17 days ago; only 1lb higher and the booking of William Maggs catches the eye, so he looks a leading contender.
runners.comment: Only 1lb higher than when a solid second over C&D last time; leading contender.

runners.name: Reagrove Lord
runners.spotlight: Has produced a couple of fair efforts in defeat, including a 10l third over C&D in this headgear combination in December 2024, but subsequent runs were disappointing; returns from a lengthy absence with major questions to answer.
runners.comment: Returns from an absence with major questions to answer.

runners.name: East Eagle
runners.spotlight: Reliable 6yo who has continued in good heart since scoring at Catterick (3m1f, good) in January and produced a career-best when back on the scoresheet at Stratford (3m2f, good) last month; failed to land a blow over C&D latest and has something to find with My Boy Christy, though he remains one for the shortlist in the context of the race.
runners.comment: Failed to land a blow over C&D latest and may find this mark demanding, but not out of it.
runners.owner: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Seeyainthesuninn
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden over hurdles but produced his best effort yet when third at Perth (2m4f, good) latest; 1lb lower now and he's one to consider if able to back that up.
runners.comment: Produced best effort yet last time and he's one to consider if backing it up.

runners.name: Valette
runners.spotlight: Has offered little more than modest form in four runs over hurdles and was beaten 25l from 1lb higher on handicap debut last month; now fitted with a tongue-tie but others make more obvious appeal.
runners.comment: Has offered little more than modest form on all hurdle starts, including on handicap debut

runners.name: Twoplacesatonetime
runners.spotlight: Unseated rider in March when last seen, but his form prior to that was underwhelming and he returns with plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Form prior to unseating latest was underwhelming which leaves him with plenty to prove.

runners.name: Bray Dale
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging start to handicap company last March but has failed to progress since and arrives here after a couple of heavy defeats; lots to prove.
runners.comment: Arrives here after a couple of heavy defeats and has lots to prove.

runners.name: Artiste d'Ainay
runners.spotlight: 2-12 in points but took ten attempts to add to his maiden success, though had earlier shown a fair level of ability over hurdles; returns to this sphere after last month's easy point win, after which he was purchased for £10,000; worth considering off a workable mark back over hurdles on stable debut.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdles on the back of a point win and on a workable mark; interesting.

races.race_name: Port Of Blyth Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Conquer The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Well supported when landing his second C&D success (good to soft) on his stable debut ten days ago following a five-month break; 8lb rise may not be enough to stop him going in again.
runners.comment: Well supported when landing his second success over C&D on his stable debut ten days ago.

runners.name: Chapel Green
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m5f at Kelso following a 163-day break last September (good) but rather disappointing since; on a good mark now, but a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won over 2m5f at Kelso last September but rather disappointing since.

runners.name: Diamond Mix
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who should strip fitter following a decent third at Newcastle (2m, good to soft) in March; still lightly raced with the promise of more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who should strip fitter following a decent third at Newcastle in March.

runners.name: Dream Jet
runners.spotlight: Has a decent record here (231413) but usually needs a run under his belt to hit form after a break; this will be his first run of the year but his course record means he cannot be ignored.
runners.comment: Usually needs a run under his belt to hit form after a break but has a decent record here.

runners.name: Chumlee
runners.spotlight: 11yo who is lightly raced for his age; never threatened on his completed starts over fences last season (fell early latest); is a dual hurdle winner but suspicion he might prove vulnerable to some of the much younger legs in this line-up.
runners.comment: Never threatened on his completed starts over fences last season.

runners.name: Don Occhetti
runners.spotlight: Scored for Dan Skelton in August 2024 but has been below form for this yard this year following a lengthy absence; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has been below form for this yard this year following a lengthy absence.

runners.name: Raffles Wonder
runners.spotlight: Won twice over fences last year for Rebecca Menzies but four lesser runs since have seen him slip down to his last winning mark; has switched stables since his last run in March and any significant move in the market should be noted.
runners.comment: Won twice over fences last year for Rebecca Menzies but four lesser runs since.

races.race_name: Port Of Blyth Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Scairp Dubh
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Carlisle in February and made a winning chase debut over C&D (good) 17 days ago; only four of the 13 managed to complete but a 3lb rise may not be enough to stop him following up.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Carlisle in February and won on chase debut over C&D 17 days ago.

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: Second in three hurdles in early 2025 but is still 0-10 under rules; pulled up here (3m, good) 17 days ago on second chase start in first-time tongue-tie (retained); the jury is out at present.
runners.comment: Pulled up here 17 days ago on second chase start in first-time tongue-tie (retained).

runners.name: Lewa House
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off 3lb higher over C&D (good to soft) last March but unable to recapture that form across his five starts last season; wears first-time tongue-tie/visor (replacing cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Runner-up off 3lb higher over C&D last March but unable to recapture that form since.

runners.name: Out On Her Own
runners.spotlight: 0-9 under rules but has been placed in her last three starts over hurdles; sports first-time hood on chase debut and interesting if she carries on the momentum in this sphere.
runners.comment: 0-9 under rules but has been placed in her last three starts over hurdles.

runners.name: Tell Me Again
runners.spotlight: 0-8 under rules; third in a couple of Irish points in October; unseated early on in race won by Scairp Dubh over C&D 17 days ago on stable debut; needs a completion in order to assess.
runners.comment: Unseated early on in race won by Scairp Dubh over C&D 17 days ago on stable debut.

runners.name: Reliance Kid
runners.spotlight: Went close in a point in May 2024 but he's struggled under rules and was well beaten at 33-1 here (3m, good) on his second chase start last month.
runners.comment: Well beaten at 33-1 here on his second chase start last month.

runners.name: Gone With The Tide
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles and out of sorts since returning last November; beaten 35l at Perth last month in first-time cheekpieces which are retained here on chase debut; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles and out of sorts since returning last November.

races.race_name: Lynn Siddall Memorial Handicap Chase

runners.name: Milan Milos
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdler but made a perfect start over fences beating three rivals to win at Plumpton (3m1f, good) last month aided by a first-time tongue-tie (retained); may well have more to offer off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Made a perfect start over fences beating three rivals to win at Plumpton last month.
runners.owner: Team Payne, Powell, Ray And Warburton

runners.name: Killybegs Jet Lady
runners.spotlight: 0-15 under rules but was runner-up over C&D on her chase debut in August and again 17 days ago; knocking on the door.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D on her chase debut in August and again 17 days ago.

runners.name: Dr Shirocco
runners.spotlight: Veteran without a win since November 2024 but showed there was still fire in the tank when third over C&D 17 days ago; younger rivals may have his measure here, however.
runners.comment: Showed there was still fire in the tank when third over C&D 17 days ago.

runners.name: Kells Priory
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles when trained in Ireland; three runs over hurdles for current yard nothing special but may be a different proposition returning to fences with blinkers replacing cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Three runs over hurdles for current yard nothing special but may improve back over fences.

runners.name: Don Brocco
runners.spotlight: 1-20 over fences and although he finished second here (4m) in March, he has run below that since at Carlisle and back over hurdles at Perth; requires an extreme test to be seen at his best.
runners.comment: 1-20 over fences; requires an extreme test to be seen at his best.

runners.name: Katie's Melody
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who has run well without winning over hurdles; still lightly raced and there should be more to come from her on her first start over fences.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who has run well without winning over hurdles; contender on chase debut.

runners.name: West Lawn
runners.spotlight: 0-19 over fences and has struggled in her last two starts, both over C&D; being 9lb wrong in the handicap doesn't help.
runners.comment: 0-19 over fences and has struggled in her last two starts, both over C&D.

runners.name: The Clonmel Kid
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his second start over fences at Newcastle (2m4f, good) last month and wasn't beaten far when last of three at Kelso (2m7f, good) 13 days ago; this looks easier and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Has made a decent start over fences, winning at Newcastle last month.

runners.name: Worth The Walk
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice at Market Rasen last season, but failed to finish on her other three starts; no easy task from 9lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Second twice at Market Rasen last season, but failed to finish on her other three starts.

races.race_name: Northumberland County Show Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Passing Diamond
runners.spotlight: Dual Irish point winner; showed marked improvement on what he'd previously achieved in three runs over hurdles when winning a maiden hurdle here over 2m this month, keeping on well; has to concede weight all round under a penalty, but his experience will help and it's likely there's more to come now going back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has a penalty to shoulder but likely there's more to come now back up in trip.

runners.name: Bold Step
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Kincardine (bumper/2m hurdle) and Steal A March (useful 2m5f-3m hurdler); made a promising debut when runner-up in an Exeter bumper in January, shaping as though this longer trip will suit now switching to hurdles; respected.
runners.comment: Promising bumper debut, should be suited by going up in trip on hurdle debut; key player.
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Mister St Eloi
runners.spotlight: Still in contention when hampered/unseating late on debut in Irish point and only narrowly denied by a subsequent hurdle winner on his second start in March last year, and later sold for £180,000; has not been seen since but is of clear interest if ready to go on rules/stable debut after a long absence.
runners.comment: £180,000 buy after finishing second in an Irish point in March last year; respected.

runners.name: Coniston Commander
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in both his bumpers, and he's likely to be suited by this step up in trip now switching to hurdles; however, there are a couple who appear to have stronger credentials on this occasion.
runners.comment: Likely to make an impact over hurdles in time, but others make more immediate appeal.

runners.name: Rachel Lopez
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; modest form in points and well beaten in a bumper; hard to gauge what she actually achieved when a 17l second to a long odds-on favourite over 2m here 10 days ago; bred to be suited by this longer distance but more likely needed in this company.
runners.comment: Bred to be suited by this longer trip but likely more is needed in this company.

races.race_name: Port Of Blyth Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Thewoodcorner
runners.spotlight: Has a largely progressive profile since winning at Chepstow (2m, soft) in January and produced a personal best when landing a Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) handicap last month, keeping on well; 6lb rise will demand more again but his age allowance is a key factor and he remains open to further progress.
runners.comment: Progressive profile and latest Ffos Las win suggests he can improve again off 6lb higher.

runners.name: Divilabother
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on stable debut in August and mainly fair form since; closely matched with Wee Alki on their clash over 3m here 17 days ago and the drop back in trip is maybe more in his favour; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair form since and closely matched with Wee Alki, respected back in trip.

runners.name: Double Digits
runners.spotlight: Largely reliable 8yo both over hurdles and fences and recent efforts in this sphere offer plenty of hope; has yet to convince he needs this far under rules though, but was a 3m soft-ground point winner and is hard to rule out should he confirm his stamina.
runners.comment: Reliable 8yo; recent hurdle runs encouraging and 3m point win offers hope for this trip.

runners.name: That One
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 2m novice hurdle here last April and follow-up effort when runner-up in a handicap at Carlisle (2m1f, soft) was promising; however, both hurdling efforts since haven't been so good and he was a mid-race faller on last month's chase debut; should stay this far and he's on a competitive mark if recapturing initial promise.
runners.comment: Made all here last April and 2nd in h'cap next time; has to recapture that initial form.

runners.name: Banderas
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell maiden hurdle (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) and a Carlisle Flat handicap (2m1f, soft) last summer, but handicap hurdle debut in September was too bad to be true; however, he made a promising return here over 2m 17 days ago when shaping as though he'd improve for the outing; interesting back up in trip.
runners.comment: Promising recent return here and interesting back up in trip if building on that effort.

runners.name: Sunset Hill
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on an encouraging return over fences in November, but latest effort over hurdles following a break was a good step forward when a close second over C&D ten days ago; nudged up 3lb for that and yet to win in handicap company, which suggests she possibly remains on a tough enough mark.
runners.comment: Likely to be in the mix but probably remains vulnerable for the win off this sort of mark.

runners.name: Wee Alki
runners.spotlight: Doubled his hurdle tally over nearly 3m here 17 days ago, but that wasn't coming out of turn after a couple of solid placed efforts; he's thriving but peak efforts have been over further and the 6lb rise asks a question.
runners.comment: Doubled hurdle score here latest and thriving, but 6lb rise over shorter trip asks more.

runners.name: Balboa
runners.spotlight: Made the most of a reduced mark when justifying support and gaining a first win since 2023 when scoring at Ayr (2m4f, soft) last month; remains well treated on past form back up 6lb and likely to be in the mix again.
runners.comment: Capitalised on a reduced mark at Ayr last time and remains well treated despite 6lb rise.

runners.name: Red Cadillac
runners.spotlight: Came good with hard-fought success on his reappearance at Carlisle (2m1f, soft) in November and has improved on that in two Ayr outings since, upped to around this trip and winning on soft last time; up 8lb which demands another career best but he has to be respected in current form.
runners.comment: Progressive since November and latest win suggests he can be competitive off 8lb higher.

runners.name: Kilbarry Hill
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in late 2024 (including a walkover) but has largely struggled off higher marks since and signed off for Olly Murphy with a couple of modest efforts, including in cheekpieces last time; 3lb lower than for his latest success but needs the change of scenery to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Completed hat-trick in 2024 but has struggled since; dangerous if revived by new yard.

runners.name: Well Educated
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner here, two over C&D on good ground (handles soft); undoubtedly on a dangerous mark but latest two efforts have been underwhelming and he needs to turn things around; safer to look elsewhere on recent form.
runners.comment: Four-time course winner but out of form recently and needs to turn things around.

runners.name: Born Like Me
runners.spotlight: Has hinted at ability but he finished a remote last in December on handicap debut at Catterick (2m4f, good), which leaves him with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Showed ability in maidens but well beaten on handicap debut and has plenty to prove.

runners.name: Terrorise
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner (1m2f/2m); highly tried on hurdles debut last April but showed an aptitude for the game when third at Market Rasen (extended 2m, good) in May and went on to land a hard-fought Cartmel success later that month; now handicapping with potential and could have more to offer up in trip, though recent Flat form tempers enthusiasm; wears a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; hurdle win at Cartmel but recent Flat form tempers enthusiasm; visored.

races.race_name: Jason Arnup After Party 6th June Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: King Kodiak
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles; has a very mixed record and is sometimes a clumsy jumper but made a bold bid from the front when third at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) two runs ago and can't be ruled out; tongue tied for first time.
runners.comment: Clumsy jumper with very mixed hurdling record; ran big race two starts ago, though

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Returned to form at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) on Wednesday, when he was reeled in after blasting off in front but kept on gamely for second; also suited by this C&D and a recent 2lb drop has now taken effect but he had a hard race at Perth and has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: Reeled in after blasting off in front at Perth on Wednesday but kept on gamely for second
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Blue Bear
runners.spotlight: Ran well for a long way and seemed to have arrested his decline when fourth at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) last month but was soundly beaten over that C&D 11 days ago and now has questions to answer again; drops back in trip with hood removed after one use.
runners.comment: Penultimate run arrested his decline but he was soundly beaten last time

runners.name: Western Soldier
runners.spotlight: Scored in pretty good style at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in November and today's 4lb higher mark is probably workable but not much has gone to plan since and he arrives here with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Not much has gone to plan since his quite stylish Wincanton win in November

runners.name: Fairly Fulling
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at 14th attempt, with clear success at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March; should be okay back at 2m on this stiff track but 7lb rise demands more from him.
runners.comment: Came good over 2m4f at Newcastle in March, his 14th hurdle start; 7lb rise demands more

runners.name: Am I Bovvered
runners.spotlight: Not beaten that far when sixth of eight on last month's handicap debut at Kelso (2m5f, good) but raced too freely (not for the first time) and was well beaten when upped to 3m at Perth three weeks later; drops back to 2m here; others look safer.
runners.comment: Not beaten that far on handicap debut (2m5f); well beaten over 3m since; now back at 2m

runners.name: Allbetsoff
runners.spotlight: Gained two of his five hurdle wins over C&D and was second off mark 7lb higher than today's when making stable debut in this race a year ago (good); lost his way afterwards, though, and absent since heavy fall in November.
runners.comment: Second in this race on stable debut a year ago but lost way afterwards; back from break

runners.name: Interchangeable
runners.spotlight: 0-5 for current stable since the autumn but seemingly aided by refitted hood and/or first-time tongue-tie when respectable third at Kelso (2m2f, good) last month and has dropped another 3lb since.
runners.comment: 0-5 for current stable but dropped another 3lb since last month's respectable Kelso third

runners.name: Fine Point
runners.spotlight: Built upon satisfactory stable debut and ran big race before weakening into third at Kelso (2m2f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; remains on a good mark and should cope with the forecast slower ground here; likely contender.
runners.comment: Ran big race before weakening into third at Kelso last month, his second stable start

runners.name: Justus
runners.spotlight: Took big step back in right direction and arguably ran better than bare form suggests when fifth at Wetherby (2m, good to soft) in March, but was generally very disappointing last season; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Latest run was step in right direction but he was generally very disappointing last term

races.race_name: Tattersalls Ireland May Hit & P2P Sale Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Edelak
runners.spotlight: 1m4f Flat winner in Ireland last summer (useful form); only sixth of seven in Listed juvenile hurdle on stable debut in December (breathing issue reported) and found one too good when 2-7 favourite at Market Rasen (2m, good; first-time cheekpieces) this month; however, he was well clear of the others on that recent outing and looks the one to beat here.
runners.comment: Beaten at skinny odds when second this month but has strong claims again here

runners.name: Crackalackin
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who won over 1m4f on Polytrack last autumn; began hurdling career with respectable third of four at Fakenham (2m, good) last month and didn't run badly before weakening into fourth at Hexham more recently; holds each-way claims at least here.
runners.comment: Fair on the Flat; ran okay on hurdling debut; may have found Hexham too stiff last time

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: Has regressed on the Flat since winning small-field Bath handicap for another stable a year ago; made fairly encouraging hurdle debut when keeping on for modest third at Plumpton (2m, good) last month but needs to build on that performance here and has since refused to race on the Flat.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging hurdle debut last month but has since refused to race on the Flat

runners.name: Garde My Guinness
runners.spotlight: Third in three good-ground bumpers in early part of last season; that form isn't anything to get too excited about and we haven't seen her since a disappointing hurdle debut in September but, nevertheless, she enters calculations.
runners.comment: Off since disappointing hurdle debut in September but was third in all three bumpers

runners.name: Trackman
runners.spotlight: 0-23 on the Flat, having had several chances off basement marks; should be fine stamina-wise over hurdles but makes very limited appeal.
runners.comment: 0-23 on the Flat, having had several chances off basement marks; hurdle debut

runners.name: Backer Bilk
runners.spotlight: Low-grade AW winner; did not achieve huge amount when well-held second of seven behind short-odds favourite at Hereford (2m, good) a fortnight ago, his second hurdle run, but might collect another minor prize here.
runners.comment: Did not achieve huge amount when well-held second at Hereford but has place claims again

runners.name: Pic N Mix
runners.spotlight: Well held in two bumpers for Michael Keady towards end of last year and also when reappearing in Uttoxeter maiden hurdle (2m good) for new stable this month.
runners.comment: Well held in both bumpers and on recent stable/hurdle debut

runners.name: Papillondelumiere
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who was soundly beaten when 100-1 for last month's hurdle debut at Southwell (2m, good).
runners.comment: Maiden pointer who was soundly beaten when 100-1 for last month's hurdle debut

races.race_name: Tattersalls Online Handicap Chase

runners.name: Kapamazov
runners.spotlight: Well beaten after racing too freely over 3m at Bangor last month and now 0-14 over fences but ran quite well when third on stable debut at Hereford (2m5f, good) in March and is suited by this track; sports refitted cheekpieces and has slipped to a good mark; dangerous to ignore.
runners.comment: Well beaten last time but ran quite well on stable debut and has slipped to a good mark

runners.name: Cloudy Wednesday
runners.spotlight: Five chase wins, including 2023 edition of this race; pulled up at Fakenham two starts ago but his other three runs this year were very respectable; swaps usual cheekpieces for first-time blinkers; likely to give this a good shot from the front.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023; three of his four runs this year were very respectable; new headgear

runners.name: Jack Doyen
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 10yo who ended last season with two 2m5f wins (good/good to soft) and posted another good effort when close second of 13 at Hexham (2m4f, good) this month; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two 2m5f wins and ran well in defeat over 2m4f this month

runners.name: Chateau Du Lys
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo; pulled up on consective appearances towards end of 2025 and was only third best on merit when battling on for modest second of six at Market Rasen (2m1f, good; first-time cheekpiecs) last month but at least that recent run gives him something to build on.
runners.comment: Only third best on merit when battling on for modest second last month; more needed here

runners.name: Pleasure Garden
runners.spotlight: Returned from layoff with two modest efforts towards end of last season (2m/2m1f) and remains without a win since 2023; yet to convince stamina-wise over this sort of trip; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Returned from layoff with two modest efforts towards end of last season; no win since 2023

races.race_name: Tattersalls Ireland May Hit & P2P Sale Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Callin Baton Rouge
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who is usually ridden patiently; didn't really have race run to suit when keeping-on fourth at Stratford (2m, good) last month and is 2lb lower here; no surprise if she poses a threat.
runners.comment: Didn't really have race run to suit when keeping-on fourth last month; 2lb lower here

runners.name: Klervia
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key stable debut when fourth of 13 in first-time cheekpieces at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month but was pulled up over that trip on next outing (given wind op soon afterwards) and it's hard to know what to expect from her back at about 2m.
runners.comment: Given wind op after last month's poor run (2m5f); back in trip here; hard to predict

runners.name: Eastern Shores
runners.spotlight: Kept on from the rear for second over C&D (soft) on handicap debut in February but was no threat to the easy winner and two lesser efforts have followed; not written off just yet but needs to learn to settle better.
runners.comment: No threat to winner when second over C&D on handicap debut; two lesser efforts followed

runners.name: Adaay Dancing
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when fourth of seven at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) in February, her second start for Max Comley, but looked out of sorts at Hereford last month and is now 0-7 over hurdles.
runners.comment: Looked out of sorts at Hereford last month and is now 0-7 over hurdles

runners.name: Alibey
runners.spotlight: Doesn't help herself by tending to race far too freely but belied 33-1 odds when close second on last month's handicap debut at Plumpton (2m, good) and has possibilities if Tabitha Worsley can keep a lid on her again here.
runners.comment: Tends to race far too freely but went close on last month's handicap debut at Plumpton

runners.name: Ride Like A Girl
runners.spotlight: Managed only fourth of eight when warm favourite for handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good) in November and not seen since but had wind surgery in March and is now fitted with a tongue-tie; hood removed; probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Only fourth when warm favourite for handicap debut but given wind op since; has potential
runners.owner: Awr Ltd Sperling Snowden Morley & Stacey

runners.name: Miracles Do Happen
runners.spotlight: Safely held after racing too freely on handicap debut in March but displayed improvement to win at Taunton (2m, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month and could easily have more to offer; good chance.
runners.comment: Scored at Taunton last month, her second handicap, and could have a lot more to offer

runners.name: Driving Miss d'Azy
runners.spotlight: Safely held at triple-digit odds in three novice/maiden hurdles (2m-2m1f; hooded for all) in the autumn but not without showing some glimmers of promise; should have improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Displayed glimmers of promise in her qualifying races; makes handicap debut today

races.race_name: Tattersalls Online Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sunray Shadow
runners.spotlight: Just 1-8 over hurdles but looked suited by the step up to 2m7f when clear second at Uttoxeter (good to soft) in March, where she travelled strongly for a long way; up in trip again here; must be considered.
runners.comment: Looked suited by step up to 2m7f when clear second at Uttoxeter in March; shortlisted
runners.owner: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate

runners.name: Ballintubber Boy
runners.spotlight: Gained the last three of his seven hurdle wins over C&D (including 2024 edition of this race); absent since two small-field seconds in October (2m5f/2m7f) but there's a good chance he'll be primed for a big run on this return to Huntingdon.
runners.comment: Absent since two small-field seconds in October but is 3-3 over C&D; likely contender

runners.name: Kitty Foyle
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with wide-margin win in three-runner contest at Wincanton (3m, good) last month; the value of that form is open to question (leader crashed through rail and ran out in home straight) but she remains unexposed over this sort of trip and the handicapper has been pragmatic in raising her 4lb.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of three-runner race last month; value of that form open to question

runners.name: Alien Storm
runners.spotlight: Better known as a chaser now; gave good account when third of five over hurdles at regular haunt Plumpton (2m4f, good) last month but has stamina to prove on first venture beyond 2m5f.
runners.comment: Ran well at Plumpton last month but has stamina to prove on first venture beyond 2m5f

runners.name: Gasmani
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement to win by 5l at Taunton (3m, good) last month, his sixth hurdling start, and ran at least at well when close second over that C&D a fortnight later; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Clear winner at Taunton last month and also ran well when second there a fortnight later

runners.name: Thank You Ma'am
runners.spotlight: Returned from layoff with three below-par chase/hurdle runs in February/March but, having swapped cheekpieces for blinkers, fared much better on last two appearances; lost out only by a nose after making most of the running at Warwick (3m1f, good) last time; can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Lost out only by a nose after making most of the running at Warwick last time
runners.owner: The Posh Pundit Racing Club Limited

runners.name: Tiger Orchid
runners.spotlight: Without a win since summer 2024 and not getting any younger but runner-up three consecutive times (2m7f-3m1f; once over fences) for new stable towards end of last season; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Runner-up all three starts for new stable towards end of last season; in the mix again

runners.name: Billy Boi Blue
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on consecutive appearances towards end of last season but seemed to run well until tiring on soft ground at Ayr last time; could be better suited by the less-testing conditions here; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Pulled up on last two outings but ran quite well until tiring on soft ground last time

races.race_name: Tattersalls Ireland May Hit & P2P Sale Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ice Jet
runners.spotlight: Looked unlucky not to make winning handicap hurdle debut at Wetherby (2m, good to soft; made bad jumping error) in March but gained compensation with 21l win at Hereford (2m3f, good to firm) a week ago; ought to be hard to beat under 7lb penalty here.
runners.comment: Won easily by 21l at Hereford last week; hard to beat under 7lb penalty here

runners.name: Breezethroughlife
runners.spotlight: Just 1-8 over hurdles but returned to form with pretty good third at Fakenham (2m7f, good) last month and didn't run badly in competitive 2m4f race there a fortnight ago; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Placed at Fakenham last month and didn't run badly in competitive race there two weeks ago

runners.name: American Trio
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first two starts under rules but rewarded market support when winning modest Exeter seller (2m2f, good) by 32l in first-time visor last month; that form is tricky to evaluate but he remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Much-improved form when winning modest seller by 32l last month

runners.name: Lightning Flash
runners.spotlight: Won twice over fences in the autumn (2m4f/2m) but looked laboured when remote third in two chases this spring; reverts to hurdling on a workable mark and with conditions suitable but needs this change of discipline to rekindle his enthusiam.
runners.comment: Looked laboured when remote third in two chases this spring; now back hurdling

races.race_name: Tattersalls Online Novices' Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Whats The Solution
runners.spotlight: 6-22 in points, the latest win gained in Friars Haugh open (2m4f, good to soft) in March; predictably struggled in Aintree Foxhunters' last month and ran out in early stages of a subsequent point but has good claims if all goes to plan here.
runners.comment: Six point wins, the latest an open this year; this is much easier than latest rules run

runners.name: On Lovers Walk
runners.spotlight: Led close home to win tactical hunter chase at Fakenham (3m, good) last month; found things happening too quickly and jumped badly right throughout when remote last of three at Warwick (2m, good) 18 days ago but moves back up in trip here and the switch to a right-handed track ought to suit.
runners.comment: Found 2m too sharp last month; has claims if judged on 3m Fakenham win in April

runners.name: Kandor
runners.spotlight: Fair handicapper when trained by Lucinda Russell and 2-6 in points for current stable, the latter win gained in three-runner mixed open at Edgcote (3m, good) last month; probably has a big role to play in this.
runners.comment: Won small-field open point last month and probably has a big role to play in this

runners.name: Surprise Attack
runners.spotlight: 6-21 in points; yet to win this year but went down fighting when close second at Witton Castle (3m, good) a fortnight ago and ought to be competitive in this.
runners.comment: 6-21 in points; close second at Witton Castle this month; ought to be competitive here

runners.name: O'Hallorans Castle
runners.spotlight: Won two 3m points last year but this year's pointing form is not as inspiring as his recent form-figures might suggest, and others in this line-up look stronger.
runners.comment: This year's pointing form is not as inspiring as his recent form-figures might suggest

runners.name: Sambezi
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdler in his prime; returned to form when dead-heating with good hunter chaser in two-runner point in March and backed up that performance with very respectable third in Garthorpe open (3m, good; tongue-tie/cheekpieces refitted) last month; one to consider.
runners.comment: Fair hurdler in his prime; back in good form in points this spring; one to consider

runners.name: J'envoievalserenki
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in points for current stable since this autumn, the latter win gained in four-runner conditions race at Mollington (3m, good to firm; first-time visor; odds-on favourite fell) this month; has more on his plate here but top amateur James King is booked again.
runners.comment: 2-7 in points for this stable since the autumn; top amateur James King booked again

races.race_name: Prix Finlande (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Valzeina
runners.owner: Ilija Steinmann, Mme Natalie Steinmann & Lucien Steinmann

runners.name: Land Of Liberty
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Hold & Tars Farm Equestrian

races.race_name: Prix Texanita (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Samangan
runners.spotlight: Smart juvenile who won two C&D Group races at this track, including when beating Street Show in the Group 2 Criterium de Maisons-Laffitte at heavy odds-on; ran well to be runner-up in the Prix Djebel (7f, good to soft) on his return but no great shock to see him back down to this trip considering his 2yo form; entered in the Commonwealth Cup and St James's Palace Stakes.
runners.comment: Won two C&D Group races as a 2yo and proved he'd trained on last time; one to beat

runners.name: Kimi Rey
runners.spotlight: Talented as a 2yo, which included a win here over 5f plus a Listed success, but performed even better this campaign, beating Street Show on her reappearance in another Listed race before finishing runner-up in a Group 3 event here, both races over 5.5f; should be okay at 6f and trainer also runs Samangan; entered in the Commonwealth Cup.
runners.comment: In good form this year; tries a new trip and stable also runs Samangan

runners.name: Street Show
runners.spotlight: Very useful as a 2yo and finished 2.5l behind Samangan in the Group 2 C&D Criterium de Maisons-Laffitte in October; goes back up to this trip after a couple of runs here over 5.5f this season, albeit behind Kimi Rey on both occasions, and should run well with cheekpieces added; first time Cristian Demuro has ridden.
runners.comment: Solid enough sprinter, but needs more for the addition of cheekpieces

runners.name: Pearl Fortune
runners.spotlight: Finished last year with a fourth in a 5f Listed event at Ayr before winning a 5.5f at the same level in October at Deauville (very soft), with Kimi Rey third; didn't figure at all on seasonal return in a race that contained a few of these, but clearly better than that; Clifford Lee back aboard.
runners.comment: Ran too badly to be true on recent return and better judged on 2yo best

runners.name: Sonatine
runners.spotlight: Well behind Pearl Fortune last October, plus Kimi Rey, in a Listed race but started this season with a win at Saint-Cloud over 7f on soft; comfortably held in a Listed event at Longchamp after that over the same trip, so needs a drop to 6f to spark some improvement.
runners.comment: Twice well held in Listed company, so others make more obvious appeal

runners.name: Dostoievsky
runners.spotlight: Had some very useful form as a 2yo, including a Listed race success here over 7f on soft ground, but below that level on his 3yo return in the Prix Djebel, when behind Samangan; did race keenly that day so drop to 6f for the first time may help.
runners.comment: Not at his best in the Prix Djebel but raced keenly and drop in trip may help
runners.owner: Alain Jathiere, Gousserie Racing & Gerard Augustin-Normand

runners.name: Kailani
runners.spotlight: Having his sixth start of the year already but been behind Kimi Rey and Street Show the last two times so, while not dismissed, clearly needs a bit extra to reverse that form; going up in trip slightly should suit.
runners.comment: Behind a couple of these the last two times so needs improvement for extra distance

races.race_name: Prix Finlande (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Valzeina
runners.owner: Ilija Steinmann, Mme Natalie Steinmann & Lucien Steinmann

runners.name: Land Of Liberty
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Hold & Tars Farm Equestrian
